basmala: In the name of God, the All-Merciful, the Compassionate.
intro: al-Aʿrāf — The Heights — is one of the great Meccan sūras: 206 verses unfolding the whole of sacred history, from the primordial Covenant to the closing call to prostration. Its name comes from the people of the Heights (al-aʿrāf), who stand upon the ramparts between the Garden and the Fire, knowing each by their mark yet awaiting their Lord's decree. The sūra rehearses the fall of Ādam and the garment of God-consciousness, the long succession of prophets — Nūḥ, Hūd, Ṣāliḥ, Lūṭ, Shuʿayb — and above all the story of Mūsā, to whom the vision was refused with the words lan tarānī, “you shall not see Me.” Qushayrī reads this refusal not as distance but as the very intimacy of the quest: the seeker is consumed before the One sought ever appears, and that consuming is itself the meeting. The sūra ends by naming the most beautiful Names of God and summoning the heart to remembrance without heedlessness.
themes: the fall of Ādam and the garment of piety
the people of the Heights between Garden and Fire
the stories of the prophets and the fate of the cities
Mūsā and the vision refused (lan tarānī)
the most beautiful Names of God

versets.ar: المص
versets.tr: alif lām mīm ṣād
versets.trad: Alif. Lām. Mīm. Ṣād.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī places these letters among the mutashābih, the veiled portion of the Qurʾān whose meaning God alone holds. Yet to the one who meditates upon them they make a sign: the alif points to the intimacy (ulfa) of fragrant souls who join together by affinity — and from this is born the union of like with like. As for the ṣād, it asks of you sincerity in love, and the mark of that sincerity is to go all the way, so that kindness does not increase it nor refusal diminish it.

mots.def: isolated letters opening the sūra, a mystery God reserves to Himself.

versets.ar: كِتَابٌ أُنزِلَ إِلَيْكَ فَلَا يَكُن فِي صَدْرِكَ حَرَجٌ مِّنْهُ لِتُنذِرَ بِهِ وَذِكْرَىٰ لِلْمُؤْمِنِينَ
versets.tr: kitābun unzila ilayka fa-lā yakun fī ṣadrika ḥarajun minhu li-tundhira bihi wa-dhikrā li-l-muʾminīn
versets.trad: A Book has been sent down to you — so let no anguish over it remain in your breast — that through it you may warn mankind, and that it may be a Reminder for the believers.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī observes that God says "in your breast" and not "in your heart": the heart of the Prophet abides in the place of contemplation, inhabited by the sweetness of nearness, and whoever tastes that sweetness knows constriction no more. For the lovers the Book is the gift of the present moment, a remedy for the pain of estrangement and a stilling of doubt.

versets.ar: اتَّبِعُوا مَا أُنزِلَ إِلَيْكُم مِّن رَّبِّكُمْ وَلَا تَتَّبِعُوا مِن دُونِهِ أَوْلِيَاءَ ۗ قَلِيلًا مَّا تَذَكَّرُونَ
versets.tr: ittabiʿū mā unzila ilaykum min rabbikum wa-lā tattabiʿū min dūnihi awliyāʾa qalīlan mā tadhakkarūn
versets.trad: Follow what has been sent down to you from your Lord, and follow no masters apart from Him. How little you reflect!
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī makes of this verse a call to total surrender: abandon yourselves to the demands of the decree, stand where He has placed you, verify what you have come to know, contemplate what has been unveiled to you; look to no other, lean on no secondary cause, and never imagine that you have, apart from Him, the slightest recourse.

versets.ar: وَكَم مِّن قَرْيَةٍ أَهْلَكْنَاهَا فَجَاءَهَا بَأْسُنَا بَيَاتًا أَوْ هُمْ قَائِلُونَ
versets.tr: wa-kam min qaryatin ahlaknāhā fa-jāʾahā baʾsunā bayātan aw hum qāʾilūn
versets.trad: How many a city have We destroyed! Our might fell upon it by night, or while they slept at noon.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: how many cities trusted in heedlessness and were seduced by the length of the reprieve; they passed the night in tranquillity and were overtaken at dawn by sudden ruin. No scourge was turned from them, no cry could save them: such is God's way with those who fall asleep upon their respite.

mots.def: the violence of the divine punishment.

versets.ar: فَمَا كَانَ دَعْوَاهُمْ إِذْ جَاءَهُم بَأْسُنَا إِلَّا أَن قَالُوا إِنَّا كُنَّا ظَالِمِينَ
versets.tr: fa-mā kāna daʿwāhum idh jāʾahum baʾsunā illā an qālū innā kunnā ẓālimīn
versets.trad: And their only plea, when Our might fell upon them, was to say: "Truly, we were wrongdoers!"
versets.commentaire: When the punishment descends upon them, their sole word is the acknowledgement of their injustice. Qushayrī notes that a confession made after the door has closed no longer profits: it is before the decree falls that one must confess one's own wrongdoing.

versets.ar: فَلَنَسْأَلَنَّ الَّذِينَ أُرْسِلَ إِلَيْهِمْ وَلَنَسْأَلَنَّ الْمُرْسَلِينَ
versets.tr: fa-la-nasʾalanna lladhīna ursila ilayhim wa-la-nasʾalanna l-mursalīn
versets.trad: We shall surely question those to whom messengers were sent, and We shall surely question the messengers too.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: none escapes the appearance before God — neither the one who received the message nor the one who carried it. God questions the peoples about their response and the prophets about their delivery, so that the proof may be complete and none be left with an excuse.

mots.def: emphatic form of the questioning at the Judgement.

versets.ar: فَلَنَقُصَّنَّ عَلَيْهِم بِعِلْمٍ ۖ وَمَا كُنَّا غَائِبِينَ
versets.tr: fa-la-naquṣṣanna ʿalayhim bi-ʿilmin wa-mā kunnā ghāʾibīn
versets.trad: We shall surely recount their story to them with full knowledge, for We were never absent.
versets.commentaire: God will relate their deeds with exhaustive knowledge. Qushayrī: "We were never absent" — His presence never deserted a single instant of their lives; the eye that knows nothing escapes Him can no longer give itself to concealment.

versets.ar: وَالْوَزْنُ يَوْمَئِذٍ الْحَقُّ ۚ فَمَن ثَقُلَتْ مَوَازِينُهُ فَأُولَٰئِكَ هُمُ الْمُفْلِحُونَ
versets.tr: wa-l-waznu yawmaʾidhin al-ḥaqqu fa-man thaqulat mawāzīnuhu fa-ulāʾika humu l-mufliḥūn
versets.trad: On that Day the weighing will be according to Truth. Those whose deeds weigh heavy, they are the ones who prosper.
versets.commentaire: The weighing will be by the Real. Qushayrī: what makes the balance heavy is not the number of works but the sincerity that dwells within them; one atom of faithfulness outweighs a mountain of empty deeds. Felicity (falāḥ) is the flowering of the one who has set his heart on the side of the Real.

mots.def: the appraisal of deeds at the Judgement.

versets.ar: وَمَنْ خَفَّتْ مَوَازِينُهُ فَأُولَٰئِكَ الَّذِينَ خَسِرُوا أَنفُسَهُم بِمَا كَانُوا بِآيَاتِنَا يَظْلِمُونَ
versets.tr: wa-man khaffat mawāzīnuhu fa-ulāʾika lladhīna khasirū anfusahum bi-mā kānū bi-āyātinā yaẓlimūn
versets.trad: But those whose deeds weigh light, they are the ones who have wrought their own ruin, because they dealt wrongfully with Our signs.
versets.commentaire: The lightness of the balance marks the loss of the soul. Qushayrī: the one who dealt wrongfully with the signs has sold his eternity for nothing; he was not so much wronged by another as he wronged himself, for nothing ruins a man so utterly as his own neglect.

versets.ar: وَلَقَدْ مَكَّنَّاكُمْ فِي الْأَرْضِ وَجَعَلْنَا لَكُمْ فِيهَا مَعَايِشَ ۗ قَلِيلًا مَّا تَشْكُرُونَ
versets.tr: wa-la-qad makkannākum fī l-arḍi wa-jaʿalnā lakum fīhā maʿāyisha qalīlan mā tashkurūn
versets.trad: We have established you firmly upon the earth and granted you therein the means of livelihood. How little you give thanks!
versets.commentaire: God recalls the gift of dwelling and sustenance. Qushayrī: every breath is a favour enfolding another favour; but the veiled eye sees the blessing only at the moment it loses it, while true gratitude is to see the Giver before the gift.

versets.ar: وَلَقَدْ خَلَقْنَاكُمْ ثُمَّ صَوَّرْنَاكُمْ ثُمَّ قُلْنَا لِلْمَلَائِكَةِ اسْجُدُوا لِآدَمَ فَسَجَدُوا إِلَّا إِبْلِيسَ لَمْ يَكُن مِّنَ السَّاجِدِينَ
versets.tr: wa-la-qad khalaqnākum thumma ṣawwarnākum thumma qulnā li-l-malāʾikati sjudū li-ādama fa-sajadū illā iblīsa lam yakun mina s-sājidīn
versets.trad: Indeed, We created you, then We shaped you, then We said to the angels: "Prostrate before Adam!" They prostrated, save Iblīs who, alone, did not.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: the angels prostrated in submission to the command, neither debating nor questioning the secret of the order; Iblīs, by contrast, set his own reasoning against the decree, and there lies his ruin. The true servant does not dispute the command: he bows before he understands, and understanding comes to him from the bowing itself.

mots.def: the refractory one, who became Satan.

versets.ar: قَالَ مَا مَنَعَكَ أَلَّا تَسْجُدَ إِذْ أَمَرْتُكَ ۖ قَالَ أَنَا خَيْرٌ مِّنْهُ خَلَقْتَنِي مِن نَّارٍ وَخَلَقْتَهُ مِن طِينٍ
versets.tr: qāla mā manaʿaka allā tasjuda idh amartuka qāla anā khayrun minhu khalaqtanī min nārin wa-khalaqtahu min ṭīn
versets.trad: God said: "What kept you from prostrating when I commanded you?" He answered: "I am better than he. You created me from fire, and him You created from clay."
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: Iblīs looked to the matter and not to the command; he judged by apparent origin — fire against clay — and forgot that nobility comes not from the element but from the chosen one. Fire rises and disperses; clay receives the breath and keeps the imprint of the divine Hand. Analogical reasoning (qiyās) set against the command is the first heresy.

versets.ar: قَالَ فَاهْبِطْ مِنْهَا فَمَا يَكُونُ لَكَ أَن تَتَكَبَّرَ فِيهَا فَاخْرُجْ إِنَّكَ مِنَ الصَّاغِرِينَ
versets.tr: qāla fa-hbiṭ minhā fa-mā yakūnu laka an tatakabbara fīhā fa-khruj innaka mina ṣ-ṣāghirīn
versets.trad: God said: "Descend from here! It is not for you to swell with pride in this place. Go forth, for you are among the abased!"
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: whoever exalts himself through pride, God abases; the greatness claimed by the creature becomes the measure of its fall. Iblīs sought to swell himself in the place of nearness, and was cast down to the rank of the most vile.

versets.ar: قَالَ أَنظِرْنِي إِلَىٰ يَوْمِ يُبْعَثُونَ
versets.tr: qāla anẓirnī ilā yawmi yubʿathūn
versets.trad: He said: "Grant me respite until the Day they are raised up."
versets.commentaire: Iblīs asks not for pardon but for a reprieve in which to lead astray. Qushayrī: the reprobate does not seek a return but the prolonging of his revolt; whereas the lover asks only to hasten the encounter, the rejected one asks only for delay, the better to do harm.

versets.ar: قَالَ إِنَّكَ مِنَ الْمُنظَرِينَ
versets.tr: qāla innaka mina l-munẓarīn
versets.trad: God said: "You are among those granted respite."
versets.commentaire: The respite is granted. Qushayrī: God makes of the reprieve itself a trial; the delay granted to the enemy is part of the decree by which the sincere and the misguided are sifted apart. Nothing, not even the cunning of Iblīs, escapes the wisdom that enfolds it.

versets.ar: قَالَ فَبِمَا أَغْوَيْتَنِي لَأَقْعُدَنَّ لَهُمْ صِرَاطَكَ الْمُسْتَقِيمَ
versets.tr: qāla fa-bimā aghwaytanī la-aqʿudanna lahum ṣirāṭaka l-mustaqīm
versets.trad: He said: "Because You have let me go astray, I shall surely lie in wait for them along Your straight path,
versets.commentaire: Iblīs casts his fault upon the decree and stations himself on the road. Qushayrī: he himself acknowledges the rectitude of the way by swearing to ambush it; the enemy attacks only where he knows the treasure lies. Let the seeker know, then, that the snares are thickest on the straightest road.

mots.sens: You have let me go astray
I shall surely lie in wait
mots.def: to station oneself to bar the way.

versets.ar: ثُمَّ لَآتِيَنَّهُم مِّن بَيْنِ أَيْدِيهِمْ وَمِنْ خَلْفِهِمْ وَعَنْ أَيْمَانِهِمْ وَعَن شَمَائِلِهِمْ ۖ وَلَا تَجِدُ أَكْثَرَهُمْ شَاكِرِينَ
versets.tr: thumma la-ātiyannahum min bayni aydīhim wa-min khalfihim wa-ʿan aymānihim wa-ʿan shamāʾilihim wa-lā tajidu aktharahum shākirīn
versets.trad: then I shall come at them from before them and from behind them, from their right and from their left. And You will not find most of them grateful."
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī notes that Satan names four directions but omits above and below: he can come neither from above — the way of prostration — nor from below — the way of humility; the servant who bows and makes himself small closes against him the only doors his cunning cannot force.

versets.ar: قَالَ اخْرُجْ مِنْهَا مَذْءُومًا مَّدْحُورًا ۖ لَّمَن تَبِعَكَ مِنْهُمْ لَأَمْلَأَنَّ جَهَنَّمَ مِنكُمْ أَجْمَعِينَ
versets.tr: qāla khruj minhā madhʾūman madḥūran la-man tabiʿaka minhum la-amlaʾanna jahannama minkum ajmaʿīn
versets.trad: God said: "Go forth from here, despised and outcast! Whoever among them follows you — I shall fill Hell with you all."
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: whoever follows the banished shares his banishment; the sentence pronounced against Iblīs extends to whoever espouses his pride. Companionship on the road makes the community of destiny, in the Garden as in the abyss.

versets.ar: وَيَا آدَمُ اسْكُنْ أَنتَ وَزَوْجُكَ الْجَنَّةَ فَكُلَا مِنْ حَيْثُ شِئْتُمَا وَلَا تَقْرَبَا هَٰذِهِ الشَّجَرَةَ فَتَكُونَا مِنَ الظَّالِمِينَ
versets.tr: wa-yā ādamu skun anta wa-zawjuka l-jannata fa-kulā min ḥaythu shiʾtumā wa-lā taqrabā hādhihi sh-shajarata fa-takūnā mina ẓ-ẓālimīn
versets.trad: "And O Adam! Dwell in the Garden, you and your spouse. Eat of its fruits wherever you wish; but do not approach this tree, lest you be among the wrongdoers."
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: the whole Garden was opened to him, and a single tree forbidden him; thus the trial never bears upon the permitted abundance but upon the single point of the prohibition, for it is there, in the realm of the no, that faithfulness is measured.

versets.ar: فَوَسْوَسَ لَهُمَا الشَّيْطَانُ لِيُبْدِيَ لَهُمَا مَا وُورِيَ عَنْهُمَا مِن سَوْآتِهِمَا وَقَالَ مَا نَهَاكُمَا رَبُّكُمَا عَنْ هَٰذِهِ الشَّجَرَةِ إِلَّا أَن تَكُونَا مَلَكَيْنِ أَوْ تَكُونَا مِنَ الْخَالِدِينَ
versets.tr: fa-waswasa lahumā sh-shayṭānu li-yubdiya lahumā mā wūriya ʿanhumā min sawʾātihimā wa-qāla mā nahākumā rabbukumā ʿan hādhihi sh-shajarati illā an takūnā malakayni aw takūnā mina l-khālidīn
versets.trad: Then Satan whispered to them, to reveal to them what had been hidden from them of their nakedness. He said: "Your Lord forbade you this tree only to keep you from becoming angels, or being among the immortals."
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: the enemy's cunning was to present the prohibition as the gateway to a higher good; he insinuated that obedience was depriving them of something. Yet whoever seeks beyond what God has allotted him falls precisely from where he thought to rise.

versets.ar: وَقَاسَمَهُمَا إِنِّي لَكُمَا لَمِنَ النَّاصِحِينَ
versets.tr: wa-qāsamahumā innī lakumā la-mina n-nāṣiḥīn
versets.trad: And he swore to them: "Truly, I am for you a sincere counsellor."
versets.commentaire: Satan swears by God in order to deceive. Qushayrī: the enemy clothes himself in the mantle of sincere counsel; his most dangerous ruse is not the threat but the feigned affection, and it is by the name of goodwill that he introduces the poison.

versets.ar: فَدَلَّاهُمَا بِغُرُورٍ ۚ فَلَمَّا ذَاقَا الشَّجَرَةَ بَدَتْ لَهُمَا سَوْآتُهُمَا وَطَفِقَا يَخْصِفَانِ عَلَيْهِمَا مِن وَرَقِ الْجَنَّةِ ۖ وَنَادَاهُمَا رَبُّهُمَا أَلَمْ أَنْهَكُمَا عَن تِلْكُمَا الشَّجَرَةِ وَأَقُل لَّكُمَا إِنَّ الشَّيْطَانَ لَكُمَا عَدُوٌّ مُّبِينٌ
versets.tr: fa-dallāhumā bi-ghurūrin fa-lammā dhāqā sh-shajarata badat lahumā sawʾātuhumā wa-ṭafiqā yakhṣifāni ʿalayhimā min waraqi l-jannati wa-nādāhumā rabbuhumā a-lam anhakumā ʿan tilkumā sh-shajarati wa-aqul lakumā inna sh-shayṭāna lakumā ʿaduwwun mubīn
versets.trad: Thus he brought about their fall by deception. When they had tasted of the tree, their nakedness became apparent to them, and they began to cover themselves with the leaves of the Garden. Their Lord called out to them: "Did I not forbid you that tree? Did I not tell you that Satan is for you a manifest enemy?"
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: the moment they tasted disobedience, the garment of grace withdrew and nakedness appeared; for the servant's adornment is not cloth but nearness, and sin lays bare even before it punishes. The Lord's call — "Did I not tell you?" — is already a call to return.

mots.racine: ع د و / ب ي ن

versets.ar: قَالَا رَبَّنَا ظَلَمْنَا أَنفُسَنَا وَإِن لَّمْ تَغْفِرْ لَنَا وَتَرْحَمْنَا لَنَكُونَنَّ مِنَ الْخَاسِرِينَ
versets.tr: qālā rabbanā ẓalamnā anfusanā wa-in lam taghfir lanā wa-tarḥamnā la-nakūnanna mina l-khāsirīn
versets.trad: They said: "Our Lord! We have wronged ourselves. If You do not forgive us and have mercy upon us, we shall surely be among the lost."
versets.commentaire: Here is the prayer of repentance that sets Ādam apart from Iblīs. Qushayrī: both disobeyed, but Ādam accused himself while Iblīs accused his Lord; the one said "we have wronged ourselves," the other said "You have led me astray." The confession of one's own fault is the first step of return, and the casting of the fault upon God is the first degree of damnation.

mots.racine: ظ ل م / ن ف س
mots.def: were unjust toward our own souls.

versets.ar: قَالَ اهْبِطُوا بَعْضُكُمْ لِبَعْضٍ عَدُوٌّ ۖ وَلَكُمْ فِي الْأَرْضِ مُسْتَقَرٌّ وَمَتَاعٌ إِلَىٰ حِينٍ
versets.tr: qāla hbiṭū baʿḍukum li-baʿḍin ʿaduwwun wa-lakum fī l-arḍi mustaqarrun wa-matāʿun ilā ḥīn
versets.trad: God said: "Descend! You shall be enemies to one another, and upon the earth you shall have a dwelling and an enjoyment for a time."
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: the earth was given only as a temporary halt; whoever plants his heart there as in an eternal abode has mistaken his address. The wise man camps there as a traveller, not as an owner.

versets.ar: قَالَ فِيهَا تَحْيَوْنَ وَفِيهَا تَمُوتُونَ وَمِنْهَا تُخْرَجُونَ
versets.tr: qāla fīhā taḥyawna wa-fīhā tamūtūna wa-minhā tukhrajūn
versets.trad: He said further: "Upon it you shall live, upon it you shall die, and from it you shall be brought forth."
versets.commentaire: Life, death, resurrection: the earth is the crucible of all three. Qushayrī: the same earth that bears you alive will receive you dead and yield you up resurrected; recognise in it, then, not your end but your passage, and make of your sojourn a seed for the final harvest.

versets.ar: يَا بَنِي آدَمَ قَدْ أَنزَلْنَا عَلَيْكُمْ لِبَاسًا يُوَارِي سَوْآتِكُمْ وَرِيشًا ۖ وَلِبَاسُ التَّقْوَىٰ ذَٰلِكَ خَيْرٌ ۚ ذَٰلِكَ مِنْ آيَاتِ اللَّهِ لَعَلَّهُمْ يَذَّكَّرُونَ
versets.tr: yā banī ādama qad anzalnā ʿalaykum libāsan yuwārī sawʾātikum wa-rīshan wa-libāsu t-taqwā dhālika khayrun dhālika min āyāti llāhi laʿallahum yadhdhakkarūn
versets.trad: O children of Adam! We have bestowed upon you garments to cover your nakedness, and adornment; but the garment of God-fearing, that is better! These are among the signs of God. Perhaps they will take heed.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: there is a garment for the body and a garment for the heart; cloth covers the outward nakedness, but the garment of reverent fear (taqwā) covers the nakedness of the soul. Blessed is he who clothes himself in both, and more blessed still is he who does not forget the second.

mots.racine: ل ب س / و ق ي
mots.def: the inner adornment of reverent fear.

versets.ar: يَا بَنِي آدَمَ لَا يَفْتِنَنَّكُمُ الشَّيْطَانُ كَمَا أَخْرَجَ أَبَوَيْكُم مِّنَ الْجَنَّةِ يَنزِعُ عَنْهُمَا لِبَاسَهُمَا لِيُرِيَهُمَا سَوْآتِهِمَا ۗ إِنَّهُ يَرَاكُمْ هُوَ وَقَبِيلُهُ مِنْ حَيْثُ لَا تَرَوْنَهُمْ ۗ إِنَّا جَعَلْنَا الشَّيَاطِينَ أَوْلِيَاءَ لِلَّذِينَ لَا يُؤْمِنُونَ
versets.tr: yā banī ādama lā yaftinannakumu sh-shayṭānu kamā akhraja abawaykum mina l-jannati yanziʿu ʿanhumā libāsahumā li-yuriyahumā sawʾātihimā innahu yarākum huwa wa-qabīluhu min ḥaythu lā tarawnahum innā jaʿalnā sh-shayāṭīna awliyāʾa li-lladhīna lā yuʾminūn
versets.trad: O children of Adam! Let not Satan tempt you as he drove your parents out of the Garden, stripping them of their garment to show them their nakedness. Truly, he and his tribe see you from where you do not see them. We have made the demons the intimates of those who do not believe.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: the enemy sees you from where you do not see him — such is the asymmetry of the combat. Your only defence is the vigilance of the heart and recourse to the One who does see the enemy. Whoever does not believe delivers himself up defenceless, and makes the demon his confidant.

mots.def: let him not lead you astray by temptation.

versets.ar: وَإِذَا فَعَلُوا فَاحِشَةً قَالُوا وَجَدْنَا عَلَيْهَا آبَاءَنَا وَاللَّهُ أَمَرَنَا بِهَا ۗ قُلْ إِنَّ اللَّهَ لَا يَأْمُرُ بِالْفَحْشَاءِ ۖ أَتَقُولُونَ عَلَى اللَّهِ مَا لَا تَعْلَمُونَ
versets.tr: wa-idhā faʿalū fāḥishatan qālū wajadnā ʿalayhā ābāʾanā wa-llāhu amaranā bihā qul inna llāha lā yaʾmuru bi-l-faḥshāʾi a-taqūlūna ʿalā llāhi mā lā taʿlamūn
versets.trad: And when they commit an indecency, they say: "We found our fathers practising it, and God has commanded it of us." Say: "God does not command indecency. Will you say of God what you do not know?"
themes: the blind imitation of the fathers
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: they dress their passion in the name of tradition and their straying in the name of God; but the custom of the fathers does not sanctify the fault, and to ascribe to God what He has not said is the gravest of inventions.

versets.ar: قُلْ أَمَرَ رَبِّي بِالْقِسْطِ ۖ وَأَقِيمُوا وُجُوهَكُمْ عِندَ كُلِّ مَسْجِدٍ وَادْعُوهُ مُخْلِصِينَ لَهُ الدِّينَ ۚ كَمَا بَدَأَكُمْ تَعُودُونَ
versets.tr: qul amara rabbī bi-l-qisṭi wa-aqīmū wujūhakum ʿinda kulli masjidin wa-dʿūhu mukhliṣīna lahu d-dīna kamā badaʾakum taʿūdūn
versets.trad: Say [O Muhammad]: "My Lord has commanded equity. Set your faces toward Him at every place of prayer, and call upon Him, devoting your worship sincerely to Him alone. Just as He brought you into being, so shall you return."
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: "just as He began you, so shall you return" — you came forth from Him stripped of all belonging, you shall return to Him divested of all but Him. Sincere worship (ikhlāṣ) is to render Him service just as you will leave this world: for Him alone, with no glance toward any other.

mots.def: pure of all association in intention.

versets.ar: فَرِيقًا هَدَىٰ وَفَرِيقًا حَقَّ عَلَيْهِمُ الضَّلَالَةُ ۗ إِنَّهُمُ اتَّخَذُوا الشَّيَاطِينَ أَوْلِيَاءَ مِن دُونِ اللَّهِ وَيَحْسَبُونَ أَنَّهُم مُّهْتَدُونَ
versets.tr: farīqan hadā wa-farīqan ḥaqqa ʿalayhimu ḍ-ḍalālatu innahumu ttakhadhū sh-shayāṭīna awliyāʾa min dūni llāhi wa-yaḥsabūna annahum muhtadūn
versets.trad: Some He has guided, while upon others error has fallen due. They took the demons as intimates rather than God, and they suppose themselves rightly guided.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: the subtlest of all straying is that which takes itself for guidance; the blind man who knows he is blind seeks a guide, but the one who believes he sees runs to his ruin without even calling for help.

versets.ar: يَا بَنِي آدَمَ خُذُوا زِينَتَكُمْ عِندَ كُلِّ مَسْجِدٍ وَكُلُوا وَاشْرَبُوا وَلَا تُسْرِفُوا ۚ إِنَّهُ لَا يُحِبُّ الْمُسْرِفِينَ
versets.tr: yā banī ādama khudhū zīnatakum ʿinda kulli masjidin wa-kulū wa-shrabū wa-lā tusrifū innahu lā yuḥibbu l-musrifīn
versets.trad: O children of Adam! Take your fair raiment at every place of prayer. Eat and drink, but do not be excessive. God does not love those who are excessive.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: the true adornment for standing before Him is not the trappings of the body but the purity of the heart; and excess lies not only in eating and drinking but in every overflow where the soul forgets the measure that God loves.

versets.ar: قُلْ مَنْ حَرَّمَ زِينَةَ اللَّهِ الَّتِي أَخْرَجَ لِعِبَادِهِ وَالطَّيِّبَاتِ مِنَ الرِّزْقِ ۚ قُلْ هِيَ لِلَّذِينَ آمَنُوا فِي الْحَيَاةِ الدُّنْيَا خَالِصَةً يَوْمَ الْقِيَامَةِ ۗ كَذَٰلِكَ نُفَصِّلُ الْآيَاتِ لِقَوْمٍ يَعْلَمُونَ
versets.tr: qul man ḥarrama zīnata llāhi llatī akhraja li-ʿibādihi wa-ṭ-ṭayyibāti mina r-rizqi qul hiya li-lladhīna āmanū fī l-ḥayāti d-dunyā khāliṣatan yawma l-qiyāmati kadhālika nufaṣṣilu l-āyāti li-qawmin yaʿlamūn
versets.trad: Say: "Who has forbidden the adornment of God, which He has brought forth for His servants, and the good things of His provision?" Say: "These are for the believers in the life of this world, and theirs alone on the Day of Resurrection." Thus do We set forth the signs clearly for a people who know.
themes: the pure enjoyment on the Last Day
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: the good things are shared in this world between believer and unbeliever, but on the Last Day they will return pure to the believers alone; thus the gift attains its full sweetness only for the one who saw the Giver within it. The believer tastes the blessing while recognising the Hand, and therein lies the portion reserved for him.

versets.ar: قُلْ إِنَّمَا حَرَّمَ رَبِّيَ الْفَوَاحِشَ مَا ظَهَرَ مِنْهَا وَمَا بَطَنَ وَالْإِثْمَ وَالْبَغْيَ بِغَيْرِ الْحَقِّ وَأَن تُشْرِكُوا بِاللَّهِ مَا لَمْ يُنَزِّلْ بِهِ سُلْطَانًا وَأَن تَقُولُوا عَلَى اللَّهِ مَا لَا تَعْلَمُونَ
versets.tr: qul innamā ḥarrama rabbiya l-fawāḥisha mā ẓahara minhā wa-mā baṭana wa-l-ithma wa-l-baghya bi-ghayri l-ḥaqqi wa-an tushrikū bi-llāhi mā lam yunazzil bihi sulṭānan wa-an taqūlū ʿalā llāhi mā lā taʿlamūn
versets.trad: Say: "My Lord has forbidden only indecencies, whether outward or hidden, and sin, and unjust violence, and that you associate with God that for which He has sent down no authority, and that you say of God what you do not know."
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: the gravest of the prohibited things is to say of God what one does not know: for indecency defiles the body, but lying about God defiles faith at its very source. Hidden faults are more to be feared than the open ones, for they elude regret as they elude shame.

versets.ar: وَلِكُلِّ أُمَّةٍ أَجَلٌ ۖ فَإِذَا جَاءَ أَجَلُهُمْ لَا يَسْتَأْخِرُونَ سَاعَةً ۖ وَلَا يَسْتَقْدِمُونَ
versets.tr: wa-li-kulli ummatin ajalun fa-idhā jāʾa ajaluhum lā yastaʾkhirūna sāʿatan wa-lā yastaqdimūn
versets.trad: For every community there is a fixed term. When its term comes, they can neither delay it nor hasten it by a single hour.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: the term is sealed in advance; none hastens it by his haste nor defers it by his fear. Know this, and you will be delivered from anxiety over what does not depend on you, and freed for what does: your conduct within the time allotted.

mots.def: a fixed span, an appointed time.

versets.ar: يَا بَنِي آدَمَ إِمَّا يَأْتِيَنَّكُمْ رُسُلٌ مِّنكُمْ يَقُصُّونَ عَلَيْكُمْ آيَاتِي ۙ فَمَنِ اتَّقَىٰ وَأَصْلَحَ فَلَا خَوْفٌ عَلَيْهِمْ وَلَا هُمْ يَحْزَنُونَ
versets.tr: yā banī ādama immā yaʾtiyannakum rusulun minkum yaquṣṣūna ʿalaykum āyātī fa-mani ttaqā wa-aṣlaḥa fa-lā khawfun ʿalayhim wa-lā hum yaḥzanūn
versets.trad: O children of Adam! Whenever messengers from among you come to you, recounting My signs, then those who fear God and amend their ways shall have no fear, nor shall they grieve.
themes: the messengers from among the people
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: "no fear shall be upon them, nor shall they grieve" — fear looks to the future, grief looks to the past; whoever lives with God in the present moment is delivered from both, for his past is entrusted to forgiveness and his future to providence.

versets.ar: وَالَّذِينَ كَذَّبُوا بِآيَاتِنَا وَاسْتَكْبَرُوا عَنْهَا أُولَٰئِكَ أَصْحَابُ النَّارِ ۖ هُمْ فِيهَا خَالِدُونَ
versets.tr: wa-lladhīna kadhdhabū bi-āyātinā wa-stakbarū ʿanhā ulāʾika aṣḥābu n-nāri hum fīhā khālidūn
versets.trad: But those who reject Our signs and turn from them in arrogance, they are the companions of the Fire; therein they shall abide forever.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: it is not ignorance that damns but the pride that turns away after having seen; denial joined to self-inflation closes the door of return, for the arrogant will not stoop to ask forgiveness.

mots.racine: ص ح ب / ن و ر

versets.ar: فَمَنْ أَظْلَمُ مِمَّنِ افْتَرَىٰ عَلَى اللَّهِ كَذِبًا أَوْ كَذَّبَ بِآيَاتِهِ ۚ أُولَٰئِكَ يَنَالُهُمْ نَصِيبُهُم مِّنَ الْكِتَابِ ۖ حَتَّىٰ إِذَا جَاءَتْهُمْ رُسُلُنَا يَتَوَفَّوْنَهُمْ قَالُوا أَيْنَ مَا كُنتُمْ تَدْعُونَ مِن دُونِ اللَّهِ ۖ قَالُوا ضَلُّوا عَنَّا وَشَهِدُوا عَلَىٰ أَنفُسِهِمْ أَنَّهُمْ كَانُوا كَافِرِينَ
versets.tr: fa-man aẓlamu mimmani ftarā ʿalā llāhi kadhiban aw kadhdhaba bi-āyātihi ulāʾika yanāluhum naṣībuhum mina l-kitābi ḥattā idhā jāʾathum rusulunā yatawaffawnahum qālū ayna mā kuntum tadʿūna min dūni llāhi qālū ḍallū ʿannā wa-shahidū ʿalā anfusihim annahum kānū kāfirīn
versets.trad: Who, then, is more iniquitous than one who forges a lie against God, or rejects His signs? Such men shall receive their portion ordained in the Book, until the moment Our envoys come to take their souls, saying: "Where now are those you called upon apart from God?" They will answer: "They have forsaken us" — bearing witness against themselves that they were disbelievers.
themes: the desertion of the false gods
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: at the hour of the soul's wrenching away, every support apart from God gives way; the idols once served vanish, and the soul stands alone before the truth of its own denial. Whoever places his trust in any other than Him finds his hands empty at the moment he most needs them full.

versets.ar: قَالَ ادْخُلُوا فِي أُمَمٍ قَدْ خَلَتْ مِن قَبْلِكُم مِّنَ الْجِنِّ وَالْإِنسِ فِي النَّارِ ۖ كُلَّمَا دَخَلَتْ أُمَّةٌ لَّعَنَتْ أُخْتَهَا ۖ حَتَّىٰ إِذَا ادَّارَكُوا فِيهَا جَمِيعًا قَالَتْ أُخْرَاهُمْ لِأُولَاهُمْ رَبَّنَا هَٰؤُلَاءِ أَضَلُّونَا فَآتِهِمْ عَذَابًا ضِعْفًا مِّنَ النَّارِ ۖ قَالَ لِكُلٍّ ضِعْفٌ وَلَٰكِن لَّا تَعْلَمُونَ
versets.tr: qāla dkhulū fī umamin qad khalat min qablikum mina l-jinni wa-l-insi fī n-nāri kullamā dakhalat ummatun laʿanat ukhtahā ḥattā idhā ddārakū fīhā jamīʿan qālat ukhrāhum li-ūlāhum rabbanā hāʾulāʾi aḍallūnā fa-ātihim ʿadhāban ḍiʿfan mina n-nāri qāla li-kullin ḍiʿfun wa-lākin lā taʿlamūn
versets.trad: God will say: "Enter the Fire among the communities of jinn and men that have passed away before you." Each time a community enters, it will curse its sister, until, when they have all been gathered together within it, the last to arrive will say of the first: "Our Lord! These are the ones who led us astray; inflict upon them a double punishment of the Fire!" He will say: "For each there is a double, but you do not know."
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: the companions of error, who loved one another in the fault, curse one another in the punishment; every bond built apart from God turns to hatred on the Day of unveiling. Only the love knit in Him endures.

versets.ar: وَقَالَتْ أُولَاهُمْ لِأُخْرَاهُمْ فَمَا كَانَ لَكُمْ عَلَيْنَا مِن فَضْلٍ فَذُوقُوا الْعَذَابَ بِمَا كُنتُمْ تَكْسِبُونَ
versets.tr: wa-qālat ūlāhum li-ukhrāhum fa-mā kāna lakum ʿalaynā min faḍlin fa-dhūqū l-ʿadhāba bi-mā kuntum taksibūn
versets.trad: And the first will say to the last: "You have no advantage over us; so taste the punishment for what you have earned."
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: each one tastes what he has earned; none bears another's burden to be rid of his own, and none excuses his fault by the one that went before. The choice to follow remains a choice for which one answers.

versets.ar: إِنَّ الَّذِينَ كَذَّبُوا بِآيَاتِنَا وَاسْتَكْبَرُوا عَنْهَا لَا تُفَتَّحُ لَهُمْ أَبْوَابُ السَّمَاءِ وَلَا يَدْخُلُونَ الْجَنَّةَ حَتَّىٰ يَلِجَ الْجَمَلُ فِي سَمِّ الْخِيَاطِ ۚ وَكَذَٰلِكَ نَجْزِي الْمُجْرِمِينَ
versets.tr: inna lladhīna kadhdhabū bi-āyātinā wa-stakbarū ʿanhā lā tufattaḥu lahum abwābu s-samāʾi wa-lā yadkhulūna l-jannata ḥattā yalija l-jamalu fī sammi l-khiyāṭi wa-kadhālika najzī l-mujrimīn
versets.trad: Truly, for those who reject Our signs and turn from them in arrogance, the gates of heaven shall not open, nor shall they enter the Garden until the camel passes through the eye of the needle. Thus do We requite the guilty.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: the gates of heaven open to the souls of the faithful as one welcomes a friend; but to the arrogant they remain shut, for pride is heavy and the passage narrow. The swollen heart cannot pass through the eye of humility.

mots.def: the animal, or the thick cable on one reading.

mots.racine: س م م / خ ي ط

versets.ar: لَهُم مِّن جَهَنَّمَ مِهَادٌ وَمِن فَوْقِهِمْ غَوَاشٍ ۚ وَكَذَٰلِكَ نَجْزِي الظَّالِمِينَ
versets.tr: lahum min jahannama mihādun wa-min fawqihim ghawāshin wa-kadhālika najzī ẓ-ẓālimīn
versets.trad: Gehenna shall be their bed, and above them coverings of fire shall enfold them. Thus do We requite the wrongdoers.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: the Fire enwraps them from below as from above; even as a man had wrapped himself in his own disobedience, so is he now draped in it. Recompense is but the unveiling of the state one had woven for oneself.

mots.def: that which covers over, scorching veils.

versets.ar: وَالَّذِينَ آمَنُوا وَعَمِلُوا الصَّالِحَاتِ لَا نُكَلِّفُ نَفْسًا إِلَّا وُسْعَهَا أُولَٰئِكَ أَصْحَابُ الْجَنَّةِ ۖ هُمْ فِيهَا خَالِدُونَ
versets.tr: wa-lladhīna āmanū wa-ʿamilū ṣ-ṣāliḥāti lā nukallifu nafsan illā wusʿahā ulāʾika aṣḥābu l-jannati hum fīhā khālidūn
versets.trad: But those who believe and perform righteous deeds — We charge no soul beyond its capacity — they are the companions of the Garden, wherein they shall abide forever.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: "We charge no soul beyond its capacity" — this is a gentleness folded within a demand; God asks not for perfection but for sincere effort, and receives the little that is offered with such love as though it were much.

mots.def: what it is able to bear.

mots.racine: ص ح ب / ج ن ن

versets.ar: وَنَزَعْنَا مَا فِي صُدُورِهِم مِّنْ غِلٍّ تَجْرِي مِن تَحْتِهِمُ الْأَنْهَارُ ۖ وَقَالُوا الْحَمْدُ لِلَّهِ الَّذِي هَدَانَا لِهَٰذَا وَمَا كُنَّا لِنَهْتَدِيَ لَوْلَا أَنْ هَدَانَا اللَّهُ ۖ لَقَدْ جَاءَتْ رُسُلُ رَبِّنَا بِالْحَقِّ ۖ وَنُودُوا أَن تِلْكُمُ الْجَنَّةُ أُورِثْتُمُوهَا بِمَا كُنتُمْ تَعْمَلُونَ
versets.tr: wa-nazaʿnā mā fī ṣudūrihim min ghillin tajrī min taḥtihimu l-anhāru wa-qālū l-ḥamdu li-llāhi lladhī hadānā li-hādhā wa-mā kunnā li-nahtadiya lawlā an hadānā llāhu la-qad jāʾat rusulu rabbinā bi-l-ḥaqqi wa-nūdū an tilkumu l-jannatu ūrithtumūhā bi-mā kuntum taʿmalūn
versets.trad: We shall have stripped from their breasts every trace of rancour. Rivers shall flow beneath their feet. They will say: "Praise be to God who guided us to this. We would never have been guided had God not guided us. The messengers of our Lord came indeed with the Truth." And it shall be called out to them: "This is the Garden bequeathed to you for what you used to do."
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: the first grace of the Garden is the tearing away of all rancour from the heart; there no resentment remains, for the purity of the abode requires the purity of the breasts. And their first word acknowledges that guidance came from Him alone — "we would never have been guided without Him" — the summit of humility within felicity itself.

mots.def: you have received it as a legacy.

versets.ar: وَنَادَىٰ أَصْحَابُ الْجَنَّةِ أَصْحَابَ النَّارِ أَن قَدْ وَجَدْنَا مَا وَعَدَنَا رَبُّنَا حَقًّا فَهَلْ وَجَدتُّم مَّا وَعَدَ رَبُّكُمْ حَقًّا ۖ قَالُوا نَعَمْ ۚ فَأَذَّنَ مُؤَذِّنٌ بَيْنَهُمْ أَن لَّعْنَةُ اللَّهِ عَلَى الظَّالِمِينَ
versets.tr: wa-nādā aṣḥābu l-jannati aṣḥāba n-nāri an qad wajadnā mā waʿadanā rabbunā ḥaqqan fa-hal wajadtum mā waʿada rabbukum ḥaqqan qālū naʿam fa-adhdhana muʾadhdhinun baynahum an laʿnatu llāhi ʿalā ẓ-ẓālimīn
versets.trad: The companions of the Garden will call out to the companions of the Fire: "We have found true what our Lord promised us; have you found true what your Lord promised you?" "Yes," they will say. Then a herald among them will proclaim: "The curse of God be upon the wrongdoers,
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: the promise of God is fulfilled entire, whether of grace or of severity; the people of the Garden taste it in sweetness, the people of the Fire in bitterness, yet all bear witness that it was true. The Real disappoints neither His friends nor His enemies.

versets.ar: الَّذِينَ يَصُدُّونَ عَن سَبِيلِ اللَّهِ وَيَبْغُونَهَا عِوَجًا وَهُم بِالْآخِرَةِ كَافِرُونَ
versets.tr: alladhīna yaṣuddūna ʿan sabīli llāhi wa-yabghūnahā ʿiwajan wa-hum bi-l-ākhirati kāfirūn
versets.trad: those who turn men away from the path of God and would have it crooked, and who deny the life to come."
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: they are not content to stray themselves; they would make the very path crooked for others. Such is the height of iniquity: not merely to refuse the light, but to labour to extinguish it in others.

versets.ar: وَبَيْنَهُمَا حِجَابٌ ۚ وَعَلَى الْأَعْرَافِ رِجَالٌ يَعْرِفُونَ كُلًّا بِسِيمَاهُمْ ۚ وَنَادَوْا أَصْحَابَ الْجَنَّةِ أَن سَلَامٌ عَلَيْكُمْ ۚ لَمْ يَدْخُلُوهَا وَهُمْ يَطْمَعُونَ
versets.tr: wa-baynahumā ḥijābun wa-ʿalā l-aʿrāfi rijālun yaʿrifūna kullan bi-sīmāhum wa-nādaw aṣḥāba l-jannati an salāmun ʿalaykum lam yadkhulūhā wa-hum yaṭmaʿūn
versets.trad: Between the two groups a veil is drawn, and upon the Heights stand men who recognize each by his mark. They call out to the companions of the Garden: "Peace be upon you!" — though they have not yet entered it, even as they long to.
themes: the veil between the two worlds
versets.commentaire: This is the verse that gives the sura its name. Qushayrī: the people of the Heights stand upon the elevation that divides, neither among the damned nor yet among the admitted; they know each by his mark, for the unveiling lays bare the inner states. Their waiting between fear and hope is itself a station, and their greeting of peace to the elect is already a promise for them.

mots.def: the crest separating Paradise and Hell.

versets.ar: وَإِذَا صُرِفَتْ أَبْصَارُهُمْ تِلْقَاءَ أَصْحَابِ النَّارِ قَالُوا رَبَّنَا لَا تَجْعَلْنَا مَعَ الْقَوْمِ الظَّالِمِينَ
versets.tr: wa-idhā ṣurifat abṣāruhum tilqāʾa aṣḥābi n-nāri qālū rabbanā lā tajʿalnā maʿa l-qawmi ẓ-ẓālimīn
versets.trad: And when their eyes are turned towards the companions of the Fire, they cry: "Our Lord, do not place us with the wrongdoing folk!"
themes: the prayer of the people of the Heights
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: the sight of another's chastisement revives their supplication; they do not reckon themselves secure, and still they implore. This fear held fast to the very threshold is the mark of the sincere: they dare not count themselves among the saved until they are.

versets.ar: وَنَادَىٰ أَصْحَابُ الْأَعْرَافِ رِجَالًا يَعْرِفُونَهُم بِسِيمَاهُمْ قَالُوا مَا أَغْنَىٰ عَنكُمْ جَمْعُكُمْ وَمَا كُنتُمْ تَسْتَكْبِرُونَ
versets.tr: wa-nādā aṣḥābu l-aʿrāfi rijālan yaʿrifūnahum bi-sīmāhum qālū mā aghnā ʿankum jamʿukum wa-mā kuntum tastakbirūn
versets.trad: From the Heights they will also call out to men whom they recognize by their marks: "Neither what you amassed nor your pride has availed you anything,
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: the heap of goods and the pride of rank weigh as nothing at the hour of unveiling; what was thought a fortune is shown to be empty, and what was despised — the poor man's humility before God — is shown to be the only true treasure.

versets.ar: أَهَٰؤُلَاءِ الَّذِينَ أَقْسَمْتُمْ لَا يَنَالُهُمُ اللَّهُ بِرَحْمَةٍ ۚ ادْخُلُوا الْجَنَّةَ لَا خَوْفٌ عَلَيْكُمْ وَلَا أَنتُمْ تَحْزَنُونَ
versets.tr: a-hāʾulāʾi lladhīna aqsamtum lā yanāluhumu llāhu bi-raḥmatin udkhulū l-jannata lā khawfun ʿalaykum wa-lā antum taḥzanūn
versets.trad: Are these the very ones of whom you swore that God would never grant them mercy? [O you, the elect,] enter the Garden: no fear shall be upon you, nor shall you grieve."
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: those whom the proud judged unworthy of mercy are precisely those whom God now calls to it; thus the scorn of men decides nothing, and the divine grace often descends where the human eye saw only weakness.

versets.ar: وَنَادَىٰ أَصْحَابُ النَّارِ أَصْحَابَ الْجَنَّةِ أَنْ أَفِيضُوا عَلَيْنَا مِنَ الْمَاءِ أَوْ مِمَّا رَزَقَكُمُ اللَّهُ ۚ قَالُوا إِنَّ اللَّهَ حَرَّمَهُمَا عَلَى الْكَافِرِينَ
versets.tr: wa-nādā aṣḥābu n-nāri aṣḥāba l-jannati an afīḍū ʿalaynā mina l-māʾi aw mimmā razaqakumu llāhu qālū inna llāha ḥarramahumā ʿalā l-kāfirīn
versets.trad: The companions of the Fire will then cry out to the companions of the Garden: "Pour upon us some water, or some of what God has provided you." They will answer: "God has forbidden both to the deniers,
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: they beg for water, but the water now denied them is only the sign of a more essential water of which they deprived themselves: mercy. Whoever turns his heart from the Source here below finds it forbidden there.

versets.ar: الَّذِينَ اتَّخَذُوا دِينَهُمْ لَهْوًا وَلَعِبًا وَغَرَّتْهُمُ الْحَيَاةُ الدُّنْيَا ۚ فَالْيَوْمَ نَنسَاهُمْ كَمَا نَسُوا لِقَاءَ يَوْمِهِمْ هَٰذَا وَمَا كَانُوا بِآيَاتِنَا يَجْحَدُونَ
versets.tr: alladhīna ttakhadhū dīnahum lahwan wa-laʿiban wa-gharrathumu l-ḥayātu d-dunyā fa-l-yawma nansāhum kamā nasū liqāʾa yawmihim hādhā wa-mā kānū bi-āyātinā yajḥadūn
versets.trad: those who took their religion for distraction and play, and whom the life of this world beguiled. This day We forget them, even as they forgot the meeting of this their Day, and because they rejected Our signs."
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: "We forget them as they forgot" — God's forgetting is no ignorance but a just abandonment: he who turns his back on the meeting finds himself, on the Day of meeting, left to himself. One is forsaken only in the measure that one has forsaken.

versets.ar: وَلَقَدْ جِئْنَاهُم بِكِتَابٍ فَصَّلْنَاهُ عَلَىٰ عِلْمٍ هُدًى وَرَحْمَةً لِّقَوْمٍ يُؤْمِنُونَ
versets.tr: wa-la-qad jiʾnāhum bi-kitābin faṣṣalnāhu ʿalā ʿilmin hudan wa-raḥmatan li-qawmin yuʾminūn
versets.trad: Yet We brought them a Book that We expounded with knowledge, that it might be a guidance and a mercy for a people who believe.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: the Book was detailed in knowledge, not by chance; each of its words is set in its place by a wisdom. Yet its guidance unfolds only for the believer, for the same light that illumines one blinds another, according to the eye that receives it.

versets.ar: هَلْ يَنظُرُونَ إِلَّا تَأْوِيلَهُ ۚ يَوْمَ يَأْتِي تَأْوِيلُهُ يَقُولُ الَّذِينَ نَسُوهُ مِن قَبْلُ قَدْ جَاءَتْ رُسُلُ رَبِّنَا بِالْحَقِّ فَهَل لَّنَا مِن شُفَعَاءَ فَيَشْفَعُوا لَنَا أَوْ نُرَدُّ فَنَعْمَلَ غَيْرَ الَّذِي كُنَّا نَعْمَلُ ۚ قَدْ خَسِرُوا أَنفُسَهُمْ وَضَلَّ عَنْهُم مَّا كَانُوا يَفْتَرُونَ
versets.tr: hal yanẓurūna illā taʾwīlahu yawma yaʾtī taʾwīluhu yaqūlu lladhīna nasūhu min qablu qad jāʾat rusulu rabbinā bi-l-ḥaqqi fa-hal lanā min shufaʿāʾa fa-yashfaʿū lanā aw nuraddu fa-naʿmala ghayra lladhī kunnā naʿmalu qad khasirū anfusahum wa-ḍalla ʿanhum mā kānū yaftarūn
versets.trad: Do they await anything but the fulfilment of what it foretells? On the Day its fulfilment comes, those who had given it no heed before will say: "The messengers of our Lord did indeed come with the Truth. Have we then any intercessors to intercede for us? Or might we be sent back, to act otherwise than we acted?" They have ruined their own souls, and what they used to invent has forsaken them.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: on the Day the truth is fulfilled, they ask either for an intercessor or for a return; but there is no return for one who has let his hour slip by, and none intercedes for one who refused the intercession of the Reminder. Belated regret does not redeem the squandered respite.

mots.def: the coming-true of what it announces.
those who plead on one's behalf.

versets.ar: إِنَّ رَبَّكُمُ اللَّهُ الَّذِي خَلَقَ السَّمَاوَاتِ وَالْأَرْضَ فِي سِتَّةِ أَيَّامٍ ثُمَّ اسْتَوَىٰ عَلَى الْعَرْشِ يُغْشِي اللَّيْلَ النَّهَارَ يَطْلُبُهُ حَثِيثًا وَالشَّمْسَ وَالْقَمَرَ وَالنُّجُومَ مُسَخَّرَاتٍ بِأَمْرِهِ ۗ أَلَا لَهُ الْخَلْقُ وَالْأَمْرُ ۗ تَبَارَكَ اللَّهُ رَبُّ الْعَالَمِينَ
versets.tr: inna rabbakumu llāhu lladhī khalaqa s-samāwāti wa-l-arḍa fī sittati ayyāmin thumma stawā ʿalā l-ʿarshi yughshī l-layla n-nahāra yaṭlubuhu ḥathīthan wa-sh-shamsa wa-l-qamara wa-n-nujūma musakhkharātin bi-amrihi a-lā lahu l-khalqu wa-l-amru tabāraka llāhu rabbu l-ʿālamīn
versets.trad: Truly your Lord is God (Allāh), who created the heavens and the earth in six days, then established Himself upon the Throne. He draws the night over the day, which pursues it swiftly. The sun, the moon and the stars are subjected to His command. His indeed are the creation and the command. Blessed be God, the Lord of the worlds!
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: "His are the creation and the command" — He has made and He governs, with no partner either in the origin or in the rule. The night pursues the day "swiftly": thus the servant's states succeed one another, contraction after expansion, all subject to the one decree. Blessed be He whose command admits of no sharing.

mots.def: upon the Throne, without graspable modality.

versets.ar: ادْعُوا رَبَّكُمْ تَضَرُّعًا وَخُفْيَةً ۚ إِنَّهُ لَا يُحِبُّ الْمُعْتَدِينَ
versets.tr: udʿū rabbakum taḍarruʿan wa-khufyatan innahu lā yuḥibbu l-muʿtadīn
versets.trad: Call upon your Lord, humbly and in secret. Truly He loves not the transgressors.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: the loftiest supplication is the secret one, withdrawn from the gaze of men; for the prayer murmured in humility is purer than the one paraded. The secret between the servant and his Lord is the sanctuary of sincerity, and to violate it by display is already a kind of excess.

versets.ar: وَلَا تُفْسِدُوا فِي الْأَرْضِ بَعْدَ إِصْلَاحِهَا وَادْعُوهُ خَوْفًا وَطَمَعًا ۚ إِنَّ رَحْمَتَ اللَّهِ قَرِيبٌ مِّنَ الْمُحْسِنِينَ
versets.tr: wa-lā tufsidū fī l-arḍi baʿda iṣlāḥihā wa-dʿūhu khawfan wa-ṭamaʿan inna raḥmata llāhi qarībun mina l-muḥsinīn
versets.trad: Do not spread corruption on the earth after it has been set aright. Call upon Him in fear and in ardent hope. The mercy of God is near to those who do good.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: call upon Him between fear and hope, on the two wings by which the bird of the heart ascends; fear alone despairs, hope alone lulls to sleep. And "His mercy is near to those who do good": it withdraws only from him who withdraws himself by his corruption.

mots.racine: خ و ف / ط م ع
mots.def: fear of chastisement, hope of grace.

versets.ar: وَهُوَ الَّذِي يُرْسِلُ الرِّيَاحَ بُشْرًا بَيْنَ يَدَيْ رَحْمَتِهِ ۖ حَتَّىٰ إِذَا أَقَلَّتْ سَحَابًا ثِقَالًا سُقْنَاهُ لِبَلَدٍ مَّيِّتٍ فَأَنزَلْنَا بِهِ الْمَاءَ فَأَخْرَجْنَا بِهِ مِن كُلِّ الثَّمَرَاتِ ۚ كَذَٰلِكَ نُخْرِجُ الْمَوْتَىٰ لَعَلَّكُمْ تَذَكَّرُونَ
versets.tr: wa-huwa lladhī yursilu r-riyāḥa bushran bayna yaday raḥmatihi ḥattā idhā aqallat saḥāban thiqālan suqnāhu li-baladin mayyitin fa-anzalnā bihi l-māʾa fa-akhrajnā bihi min kulli th-thamarāti kadhālika nukhriju l-mawtā laʿallakum tadhakkarūn
versets.trad: It is He who sends the winds as harbingers of His Mercy. Thus, when they bear a heavy-laden cloud, We drive it to a dead land and send down water by which We bring forth fruits of every kind. In like manner We shall bring forth the dead — that perhaps you may remember.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: the dead land that greens again beneath the rain is the mirror of the dead heart that mercy revives; as He makes fruits spring from the barren soil, He makes faith spring from the withered heart. Whoever contemplates the spring has already before his eyes the proof of his own resurrection.

mots.racine: ب ل د / م و ت

versets.ar: وَالْبَلَدُ الطَّيِّبُ يَخْرُجُ نَبَاتُهُ بِإِذْنِ رَبِّهِ ۖ وَالَّذِي خَبُثَ لَا يَخْرُجُ إِلَّا نَكِدًا ۚ كَذَٰلِكَ نُصَرِّفُ الْآيَاتِ لِقَوْمٍ يَشْكُرُونَ
versets.tr: wa-l-baladu ṭ-ṭayyibu yakhruju nabātuhu bi-idhni rabbihi wa-lladhī khabutha lā yakhruju illā nakidan kadhālika nuṣarrifu l-āyāti li-qawmin yashkurūn
versets.trad: From the good land its vegetation springs forth in vigour by its Lord's leave, while the bad land yields nothing but a stunted growth. Thus do We vary the signs for a people who give thanks.
themes: the good and the bad land
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: hearts are lands: the pure heart receives the seed of the Reminder and yields an abundant harvest, while the corrupt heart, watered by the same water, brings forth only stunted fruit. The same grace descends upon all; it is the soil that makes the difference.

versets.ar: لَقَدْ أَرْسَلْنَا نُوحًا إِلَىٰ قَوْمِهِ فَقَالَ يَا قَوْمِ اعْبُدُوا اللَّهَ مَا لَكُم مِّنْ إِلَٰهٍ غَيْرُهُ إِنِّي أَخَافُ عَلَيْكُمْ عَذَابَ يَوْمٍ عَظِيمٍ
versets.tr: la-qad arsalnā nūḥan ilā qawmihi fa-qāla yā qawmi-ʿbudū llāha mā lakum min ilāhin ghayruhu innī akhāfu ʿalaykum ʿadhāba yawmin ʿaẓīm
versets.trad: We sent Noah to his people. He said: "O my people! Worship God! You have no god other than Him. Truly I fear for you the chastisement of a tremendous Day."
versets.commentaire: Here begins the cycle of the prophets, all bearers of the same message. Qushayrī: every messenger opens with the selfsame word — "worship God, you have no god other than Him" — for tawḥīd is the single root of which all the laws are the branches. Nūḥ's fear for his people is the love of the guide who trembles for those he calls.

versets.ar: قَالَ الْمَلَأُ مِن قَوْمِهِ إِنَّا لَنَرَاكَ فِي ضَلَالٍ مُّبِينٍ
versets.tr: qāla l-malaʾu min qawmihi innā la-narāka fī ḍalālin mubīn
versets.trad: The notables of his people said: "We see you in manifest error."
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: it is always the notables (al-malaʾ), drunk on their rank, who are the first to call the prophet astray; for the truth that abases their pride strikes them as folly. They name the very guidance error, so wholly does the veil of self-conceit invert their vision.

mots.racine: ض ل ل / ب ي ن

versets.ar: قَالَ يَا قَوْمِ لَيْسَ بِي ضَلَالَةٌ وَلَٰكِنِّي رَسُولٌ مِّن رَّبِّ الْعَالَمِينَ
versets.tr: qāla yā qawmi laysa bī ḍalālatun wa-lākinnī rasūlun min rabbi l-ʿālamīn
versets.trad: He said: "O my people! There is no error in me; rather I am a messenger from the Lord of the worlds.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: the prophet justifies himself not by himself but by Him who sends him; he does not say "I am wise" but "I am a messenger." His whole authority rests in his effacement before Him whose word he bears.

mots.racine: ر ب ب / ع ل م

versets.ar: أُبَلِّغُكُمْ رِسَالَاتِ رَبِّي وَأَنصَحُ لَكُمْ وَأَعْلَمُ مِنَ اللَّهِ مَا لَا تَعْلَمُونَ
versets.tr: uballighukum risālāti rabbī wa-anṣaḥu lakum wa-aʿlamu mina llāhi mā lā taʿlamūn
versets.trad: I convey to you the messages of my Lord, I counsel you sincerely, and I know from God what you do not know.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: three charges of the prophet: to convey the message, to counsel sincerely, and to hold from God a knowledge hidden from others. Sincere counsel (naṣīḥa) is the love that wills nothing but the good of the one it calls, even against his will.

versets.ar: أَوَعَجِبْتُمْ أَن جَاءَكُمْ ذِكْرٌ مِّن رَّبِّكُمْ عَلَىٰ رَجُلٍ مِّنكُمْ لِيُنذِرَكُمْ وَلِتَتَّقُوا وَلَعَلَّكُمْ تُرْحَمُونَ
versets.tr: a-wa-ʿajibtum an jāʾakum dhikrun min rabbikum ʿalā rajulin minkum li-yundhirakum wa-li-tattaqū wa-laʿallakum turḥamūn
versets.trad: Do you marvel that a Reminder should reach you from your Lord through one of yourselves, sent to warn you, to summon you to the fear of God, and that perhaps mercy may be shown you?"
themes: the messenger from among the people
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: they marvel that the messenger should be a man like themselves, but that is precisely the mercy: God sends not a terrifying angel but an accessible fellow, that the warning may issue in compassion and not in dread.

versets.ar: فَكَذَّبُوهُ فَأَنجَيْنَاهُ وَالَّذِينَ مَعَهُ فِي الْفُلْكِ وَأَغْرَقْنَا الَّذِينَ كَذَّبُوا بِآيَاتِنَا ۚ إِنَّهُمْ كَانُوا قَوْمًا عَمِينَ
versets.tr: fa-kadhdhabūhu fa-anjaynāhu wa-lladhīna maʿahu fī l-fulki wa-aghraqnā lladhīna kadhdhabū bi-āyātinā innahum kānū qawman ʿamīn
versets.trad: But they cried him a liar. So We saved him, and those with him, in the ark, and We drowned those who rejected Our signs. They were indeed a blind people!
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: "a blind people" — blind not of eye but of heart; they saw the signs yet did not see them. The ark that saved Nūḥ is the image of the faith that bears the believer over the floods of denial in which the blind perish.

versets.ar: وَإِلَىٰ عَادٍ أَخَاهُمْ هُودًا ۗ قَالَ يَا قَوْمِ اعْبُدُوا اللَّهَ مَا لَكُم مِّنْ إِلَٰهٍ غَيْرُهُ ۚ أَفَلَا تَتَّقُونَ
versets.tr: wa-ilā ʿādin akhāhum hūdan qāla yā qawmi-ʿbudū llāha mā lakum min ilāhin ghayruhu a-fa-lā tattaqūn
versets.trad: And to the 'Ād We sent their brother Hūd. He said: "O my people! Worship God! You have no god other than Him. Will you not fear Him?"
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: Hūd is called "their brother" — the prophet is near to his people by blood as by solicitude; he comes not as a stranger judging from on high, but as a brother who shares and calls. The same tawḥīd is spoken anew, timeless.

versets.ar: قَالَ الْمَلَأُ الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا مِن قَوْمِهِ إِنَّا لَنَرَاكَ فِي سَفَاهَةٍ وَإِنَّا لَنَظُنُّكَ مِنَ الْكَاذِبِينَ
versets.tr: qāla l-malaʾu lladhīna kafarū min qawmihi innā la-narāka fī safāhatin wa-innā la-naẓunnuka mina l-kādhibīn
versets.trad: The notables of his people who disbelieved said: "We see you sunk in folly, and we deem you indeed one of the liars!"
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: they call folly what exceeds their narrow reason; the prophetic wisdom seems madness to one who measures all by the yardstick of his passions. To invert the names thus — to call wisdom folly — is the very mark of the veiled heart.

versets.ar: قَالَ يَا قَوْمِ لَيْسَ بِي سَفَاهَةٌ وَلَٰكِنِّي رَسُولٌ مِّن رَّبِّ الْعَالَمِينَ
versets.tr: qāla yā qawmi laysa bī safāhatun wa-lākinnī rasūlun min rabbi l-ʿālamīn
versets.trad: He said: "O my people! There is no folly in me; rather I am a messenger from the Lord of the worlds.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: like Nūḥ before him, Hūd answers not with anger but with patience, returning the accusation to its source: "I am a messenger." The prophet's steadfastness in the face of insult is the signature of his truthfulness.

versets.ar: أُبَلِّغُكُمْ رِسَالَاتِ رَبِّي وَأَنَا لَكُمْ نَاصِحٌ أَمِينٌ
versets.tr: uballighukum risālāti rabbī wa-anā lakum nāṣiḥun amīn
versets.trad: I convey to you the messages of my Lord, and I am for you a trustworthy counsellor.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: "a trustworthy counsellor (amīn)" — fidelity (amāna) is the mark of the true guide; he betrays neither the message he bears nor those to whom he bears it. His word has no interest other than theirs.

versets.ar: أَوَعَجِبْتُمْ أَن جَاءَكُمْ ذِكْرٌ مِّن رَّبِّكُمْ عَلَىٰ رَجُلٍ مِّنكُمْ لِيُنذِرَكُمْ ۚ وَاذْكُرُوا إِذْ جَعَلَكُمْ خُلَفَاءَ مِن بَعْدِ قَوْمِ نُوحٍ وَزَادَكُمْ فِي الْخَلْقِ بَسْطَةً ۖ فَاذْكُرُوا آلَاءَ اللَّهِ لَعَلَّكُمْ تُفْلِحُونَ
versets.tr: a-wa-ʿajibtum an jāʾakum dhikrun min rabbikum ʿalā rajulin minkum li-yundhirakum wa-dhkurū idh jaʿalakum khulafāʾa min baʿdi qawmi nūḥin wa-zādakum fī l-khalqi basṭatan fa-dhkurū ālāʾa llāhi laʿallakum tufliḥūn
versets.trad: Do you marvel that a Reminder should reach you from your Lord through one of yourselves, sent to warn you? Remember that He made you successors after the people of Noah and gave you ample stature in creation. Remember, then, the bounties of God — that perhaps you may prosper."
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: "remember the bounties of God" — the remembrance of graces (dhikr al-ālāʾ) is the gate of gratitude, and gratitude is the gate of felicity. Hūd brings them back to their debt: they hold their strength and their stature by a gift, not by a right.

versets.ar: قَالُوا أَجِئْتَنَا لِنَعْبُدَ اللَّهَ وَحْدَهُ وَنَذَرَ مَا كَانَ يَعْبُدُ آبَاؤُنَا ۖ فَأْتِنَا بِمَا تَعِدُنَا إِن كُنتَ مِنَ الصَّادِقِينَ
versets.tr: qālū a-jiʾtanā li-naʿbuda llāha waḥdahu wa-nadhara mā kāna yaʿbudu ābāʾunā fa-ʾtinā bimā taʿidunā in kunta mina ṣ-ṣādiqīn
versets.trad: They said: "Have you come to us that we should worship God alone and forsake what our fathers worshipped? Bring us, then, what you threaten us with, if you are among the truthful!"
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: attachment to the gods of the fathers is the most stubborn veil; they defy the prophet to hasten the chastisement rather than abandon their custom. The haste to provoke severity betrays the blindness of one who prefers his habit to his salvation.

versets.ar: قَالَ قَدْ وَقَعَ عَلَيْكُم مِّن رَّبِّكُمْ رِجْسٌ وَغَضَبٌ ۖ أَتُجَادِلُونَنِي فِي أَسْمَاءٍ سَمَّيْتُمُوهَا أَنتُمْ وَآبَاؤُكُم مَّا نَزَّلَ اللَّهُ بِهَا مِن سُلْطَانٍ ۚ فَانتَظِرُوا إِنِّي مَعَكُم مِّنَ الْمُنتَظِرِينَ
versets.tr: qāla qad waqaʿa ʿalaykum min rabbikum rijsun wa-ghaḍabun a-tujādilūnanī fī asmāʾin sammaytumūhā antum wa-ābāʾukum mā nazzala llāhu bihā min sulṭānin fa-ntaẓirū innī maʿakum mina l-muntaẓirīn
versets.trad: Hūd said: "Defilement and wrath from your Lord have already fallen upon you! Will you dispute with me over names that you and your fathers have given to deities for which God has sent down no warrant? Wait, then! I too am waiting with you."
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: "names which you have named" — the idols are but empty names, shells with no god inside. Hūd unveils that idolatry worships hollow appellations; there is no real Named One but God, and the names men invent contain no presence at all.

versets.ar: فَأَنجَيْنَاهُ وَالَّذِينَ مَعَهُ بِرَحْمَةٍ مِّنَّا وَقَطَعْنَا دَابِرَ الَّذِينَ كَذَّبُوا بِآيَاتِنَا ۖ وَمَا كَانُوا مُؤْمِنِينَ
versets.tr: fa-anjaynāhu wa-lladhīna maʿahu bi-raḥmatin minnā wa-qaṭaʿnā dābira lladhīna kadhdhabū bi-āyātinā wa-mā kānū muʾminīn
versets.trad: So We saved him, and those with him, by a mercy from Us, and We cut off to the last man those who rejected Our signs and were not believers.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: "by a mercy from Us" — the salvation of Hūd and his folk is not the price of their works but the pure effect of grace; none is saved by his merit alone, but by the mercy that outstrips the merit and crowns it.

mots.racine: ق ط ع / د ب ر

versets.ar: وَإِلَىٰ ثَمُودَ أَخَاهُمْ صَالِحًا ۗ قَالَ يَا قَوْمِ اعْبُدُوا اللَّهَ مَا لَكُم مِّنْ إِلَٰهٍ غَيْرُهُ ۖ قَدْ جَاءَتْكُم بَيِّنَةٌ مِّن رَّبِّكُمْ ۖ هَٰذِهِ نَاقَةُ اللَّهِ لَكُمْ آيَةً ۖ فَذَرُوهَا تَأْكُلْ فِي أَرْضِ اللَّهِ ۖ وَلَا تَمَسُّوهَا بِسُوءٍ فَيَأْخُذَكُمْ عَذَابٌ أَلِيمٌ
versets.tr: wa-ilā thamūda akhāhum ṣāliḥan qāla yā qawmi-ʿbudū llāha mā lakum min ilāhin ghayruhu qad jāʾatkum bayyinatun min rabbikum hādhihi nāqatu llāhi lakum āyatan fa-dharūhā taʾkul fī arḍi llāhi wa-lā tamassūhā bi-sūʾin fa-yaʾkhudhakum ʿadhābun alīm
versets.trad: And to the Thamūd We sent their brother Ṣāliḥ. He said: "O my people! Worship God! You have no god other than Him. A clear proof has already come to you from your Lord: this is the she-camel of God, a sign for you. So let her graze on God's earth, and do her no harm, lest a painful chastisement seize you."
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: the she-camel is called "the she-camel of God" not that He has need of her, but to honour her as a sign; every creature assigned to Him becomes sacred. The trial of the Thamūd hangs on a single prohibition: to let her live. As with Ādam, fidelity is measured by the keeping of one sole limit.

mots.def: a she-camel consecrated as a sign.

versets.ar: وَاذْكُرُوا إِذْ جَعَلَكُمْ خُلَفَاءَ مِن بَعْدِ عَادٍ وَبَوَّأَكُمْ فِي الْأَرْضِ تَتَّخِذُونَ مِن سُهُولِهَا قُصُورًا وَتَنْحِتُونَ الْجِبَالَ بُيُوتًا ۖ فَاذْكُرُوا آلَاءَ اللَّهِ وَلَا تَعْثَوْا فِي الْأَرْضِ مُفْسِدِينَ
versets.tr: wa-dhkurū idh jaʿalakum khulafāʾa min baʿdi ʿādin wa-bawwaʾakum fī l-arḍi tattakhidhūna min suhūlihā quṣūran wa-tanḥitūna l-jibāla buyūtan fa-dhkurū ālāʾa llāhi wa-lā taʿthaw fī l-arḍi mufsidīn
versets.trad: Remember that He made you successors after the 'Ād and established you firmly on the earth. In its plains you raise palaces, and in the mountains you hew out dwellings. Remember, then, the bounties of God, and do not go about the earth as bringers of corruption.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: mastery of the arts — to hew stone, to raise palaces — is a gift that turns to pride if one forgets the Giver. Ṣāliḥ binds the remembrance of bounties to the prohibition of corruption: whoever truly remembers the grace could never defile the earth.

versets.ar: قَالَ الْمَلَأُ الَّذِينَ اسْتَكْبَرُوا مِن قَوْمِهِ لِلَّذِينَ اسْتُضْعِفُوا لِمَنْ آمَنَ مِنْهُمْ أَتَعْلَمُونَ أَنَّ صَالِحًا مُّرْسَلٌ مِّن رَّبِّهِ ۚ قَالُوا إِنَّا بِمَا أُرْسِلَ بِهِ مُؤْمِنُونَ
versets.tr: qāla l-malaʾu lladhīna stakbarū min qawmihi li-lladhīna stuḍʿifū li-man āmana minhum a-taʿlamūna anna ṣāliḥan mursalun min rabbihi qālū innā bimā ursila bihi muʾminūn
versets.trad: The notables of his people, swollen with pride, said to those who were deemed weak among them, those who had believed: "Do you really know that Ṣāliḥ is sent from his Lord?" They answered: "Truly, we believe in that with which he was sent!"
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: the "weak" in the eyes of the proud are the strong in the eyes of God; their answer — "we believe" — is without hesitation, while the powerful waver. Faith often descends first upon the humble, for their heart is unencumbered by self-conceit.

versets.ar: قَالَ الَّذِينَ اسْتَكْبَرُوا إِنَّا بِالَّذِي آمَنتُم بِهِ كَافِرُونَ
versets.tr: qāla lladhīna stakbarū innā bi-lladhī āmantum bihi kāfirūn
versets.trad: The proud retorted: "That in which you believe, we reject!"
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: pride refuses to follow in the footsteps of the humble; it prefers ruin to the humiliation of following those it despises. The veil of self-conceit is thicker than that of ignorance, for it persists even after it has seen.

versets.ar: فَعَقَرُوا النَّاقَةَ وَعَتَوْا عَنْ أَمْرِ رَبِّهِمْ وَقَالُوا يَا صَالِحُ ائْتِنَا بِمَا تَعِدُنَا إِن كُنتَ مِنَ الْمُرْسَلِينَ
versets.tr: fa-ʿaqarū n-nāqata wa-ʿataw ʿan amri rabbihim wa-qālū yā ṣāliḥu-ʾtinā bimā taʿidunā in kunta mina l-mursalīn
versets.trad: Then they hamstrung the she-camel, in rebellion against the command of their Lord, and said: "O Ṣāliḥ! Bring upon us what you have threatened us with, if you are truly one of the messengers."
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: in slaughtering the she-camel they crossed the one prohibition and sealed their ruin; and in insolence they demanded the chastisement they were denying. Such is excess: not content to transgress, it provokes the severity it deems impossible.

versets.ar: فَأَخَذَتْهُمُ الرَّجْفَةُ فَأَصْبَحُوا فِي دَارِهِمْ جَاثِمِينَ
versets.tr: fa-akhadhathumu r-rajfatu fa-aṣbaḥū fī dārihim jāthimīn
versets.trad: So the cataclysm seized them, and the morning found them lying prostrate in their dwellings.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: the quake seized them unawares; those who defied the chastisement at evening met it at dawn, frozen in their houses. The respite was but the long rope let out before it is seized back in a single jerk.

versets.ar: فَتَوَلَّىٰ عَنْهُمْ وَقَالَ يَا قَوْمِ لَقَدْ أَبْلَغْتُكُمْ رِسَالَةَ رَبِّي وَنَصَحْتُ لَكُمْ وَلَٰكِن لَّا تُحِبُّونَ النَّاصِحِينَ
versets.tr: fa-tawallā ʿanhum wa-qāla yā qawmi la-qad ablaghtukum risālata rabbī wa-naṣaḥtu lakum wa-lākin lā tuḥibbūna n-nāṣiḥīn
versets.trad: Then Ṣāliḥ turned away from them, saying: "O my people! I delivered to you the message of my Lord and counselled you well; but you love not those who counsel."
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: "you love not those who counsel" — here is the diagnosis of all perdition: the sick man who hates his physician. The prophet departs with a clear conscience, having conveyed and counselled; the blood of their ruin falls on themselves alone.

versets.ar: وَلُوطًا إِذْ قَالَ لِقَوْمِهِ أَتَأْتُونَ الْفَاحِشَةَ مَا سَبَقَكُم بِهَا مِنْ أَحَدٍ مِّنَ الْعَالَمِينَ
versets.tr: wa-lūṭan idh qāla li-qawmihi a-taʾtūna l-fāḥishata mā sabaqakum bihā min aḥadin mina l-ʿālamīn
versets.trad: And Lot, when he said to his people: "Do you commit an abomination such as no one in all the worlds ever committed before you?
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: Lūṭ denounces an abomination without precedent; novelty in evil is itself an aggravation, for it adds to the sin the audacity of the unheard-of. Here nature itself is outraged, not the law alone.

versets.ar: إِنَّكُمْ لَتَأْتُونَ الرِّجَالَ شَهْوَةً مِّن دُونِ النِّسَاءِ ۚ بَلْ أَنتُمْ قَوْمٌ مُّسْرِفُونَ
versets.tr: innakum la-taʾtūna r-rijāla shahwatan min dūni n-nisāʾi bal antum qawmun musrifūn
versets.trad: You approach men with lust in preference to women. Indeed, you are a people gone astray in excess.”
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: “a people gone astray in excess (musrif)” — excess lies not only in quantity but in direction: to disturb the order God has set is to overstep the very measure of nature. The transgression here is not in too much but in the out-of-place.

versets.ar: وَمَا كَانَ جَوَابَ قَوْمِهِ إِلَّا أَن قَالُوا أَخْرِجُوهُم مِّن قَرْيَتِكُمْ ۖ إِنَّهُمْ أُنَاسٌ يَتَطَهَّرُونَ
versets.tr: wa-mā kāna jawāba qawmihi illā an qālū akhrijūhum min qaryatikum innahum unāsun yataṭahharūn
versets.trad: His people’s only answer was to say: “Drive them out of your town; they are folk who make a show of purity.”
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: the height of reversal: they would expel the pure in the name of that very purity, making virtue a crime. When evil reigns, it is goodness that becomes the stranger and is banished; the inverted world persecutes that which would save it.

versets.ar: فَأَنجَيْنَاهُ وَأَهْلَهُ إِلَّا امْرَأَتَهُ كَانَتْ مِنَ الْغَابِرِينَ
versets.tr: fa-anjaynāhu wa-ahlahu illā mraʾatahu kānat mina l-ghābirīn
versets.trad: So We delivered him and his household, save his wife, who was among those who stayed behind.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: nearness to the prophet does not save one who refuses to believe: Lūṭ’s wife, for all the marriage bond, perished with the lost. The tie of blood or hearth counts for nothing before the tie of faith; none is saved by proxy.

versets.ar: وَأَمْطَرْنَا عَلَيْهِم مَّطَرًا ۖ فَانظُرْ كَيْفَ كَانَ عَاقِبَةُ الْمُجْرِمِينَ
versets.tr: wa-amṭarnā ʿalayhim maṭaran fa-nẓur kayfa kāna ʿāqibatu l-mujrimīn
versets.trad: And We rained down upon them a rain [of stones]. See, then, what was the end of the guilty!
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: “see what was the end” — the call to contemplate the ruins is no idle curiosity but a lesson; the accounts of chastisement are mirrors held up to the present, that the living may read in the fate of the dead his own path.

versets.ar: وَإِلَىٰ مَدْيَنَ أَخَاهُمْ شُعَيْبًا ۗ قَالَ يَا قَوْمِ اعْبُدُوا اللَّهَ مَا لَكُم مِّنْ إِلَٰهٍ غَيْرُهُ ۖ قَدْ جَاءَتْكُم بَيِّنَةٌ مِّن رَّبِّكُمْ ۖ فَأَوْفُوا الْكَيْلَ وَالْمِيزَانَ وَلَا تَبْخَسُوا النَّاسَ أَشْيَاءَهُمْ وَلَا تُفْسِدُوا فِي الْأَرْضِ بَعْدَ إِصْلَاحِهَا ۚ ذَٰلِكُمْ خَيْرٌ لَّكُمْ إِن كُنتُم مُّؤْمِنِينَ
versets.tr: wa-ilā madyana akhāhum shuʿayban qāla yā qawmi-ʿbudū llāha mā lakum min ilāhin ghayruhu qad jāʾatkum bayyinatun min rabbikum fa-awfū l-kayla wa-l-mīzāna wa-lā tabkhasū n-nāsa ashyāʾahum wa-lā tufsidū fī l-arḍi baʿda iṣlāḥihā dhālikum khayrun lakum in kuntum muʾminīn
versets.trad: And to Madyan We sent their brother Shuʿayb. He said: “O my people! Worship God! You have no god other than Him. A clear proof has already come to you from your Lord. So give full measure and full weight; do not defraud people of their goods; and do not spread corruption upon the earth after it has been set right. That is better for you, if you are believers.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: Shuʿayb joins tawḥīd to the justice of the scales: to worship God and to weigh truly are one and the same movement, for fraud in measure is a lie enacted, and idolatry a lie believed. To honour God is to honour your neighbour’s right down to the least ounce.

mots.racine: و ف ي / ك ي ل

mots.def: do not diminish what is owed.

versets.ar: وَلَا تَقْعُدُوا بِكُلِّ صِرَاطٍ تُوعِدُونَ وَتَصُدُّونَ عَن سَبِيلِ اللَّهِ مَنْ آمَنَ بِهِ وَتَبْغُونَهَا عِوَجًا ۚ وَاذْكُرُوا إِذْ كُنتُمْ قَلِيلًا فَكَثَّرَكُمْ ۖ وَانظُرُوا كَيْفَ كَانَ عَاقِبَةُ الْمُفْسِدِينَ
versets.tr: wa-lā taqʿudū bi-kulli ṣirāṭin tūʿidūna wa-taṣuddūna ʿan sabīli llāhi man āmana bihi wa-tabghūnahā ʿiwajan wa-dhkurū idh kuntum qalīlan fa-kaththarakum wa-nẓurū kayfa kāna ʿāqibatu l-mufsidīn
versets.trad: And do not lie in wait on every road, threatening and barring from the path of God those who believe in Him, seeking to make that way crooked. Remember when you were few and He made you many. And see what was the end of those who spread corruption!
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: to lurk on the roads and turn the believers away is to imitate the very ambush of Satan in verse 16; the corrupters reenact the posture of the enemy. And Shuʿayb reminds them of their early fewness: he who recalls having once been few dares not swell with pride.

versets.ar: وَإِن كَانَ طَائِفَةٌ مِّنكُمْ آمَنُوا بِالَّذِي أُرْسِلْتُ بِهِ وَطَائِفَةٌ لَّمْ يُؤْمِنُوا فَاصْبِرُوا حَتَّىٰ يَحْكُمَ اللَّهُ بَيْنَنَا ۚ وَهُوَ خَيْرُ الْحَاكِمِينَ
versets.tr: wa-in kāna ṭāʾifatun minkum āmanū bi-lladhī ursiltu bihi wa-ṭāʾifatun lam yuʾminū fa-ṣbirū ḥattā yaḥkuma llāhu baynanā wa-huwa khayru l-ḥākimīn
versets.trad: And if a party among you has believed in the Message with which I was sent, and a party has not believed, then be patient until God judges between us, for He is the best of judges.”
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: “be patient until God judges” — the prophet hands the verdict over to God and holds fast to patience; he does not make himself executioner of what belongs to the sole Judge. The believer’s serenity rests in entrusting the outcome to the One who judges best.

versets.ar: قَالَ الْمَلَأُ الَّذِينَ اسْتَكْبَرُوا مِن قَوْمِهِ لَنُخْرِجَنَّكَ يَا شُعَيْبُ وَالَّذِينَ آمَنُوا مَعَكَ مِن قَرْيَتِنَا أَوْ لَتَعُودُنَّ فِي مِلَّتِنَا ۚ قَالَ أَوَلَوْ كُنَّا كَارِهِينَ
versets.tr: qāla l-malaʾu lladhīna stakbarū min qawmihi la-nukhrijannaka yā shuʿaybu wa-lladhīna āmanū maʿaka min qaryatinā aw la-taʿūdunna fī millatinā qāla a-wa-law kunnā kārihīn
versets.trad: The notables of his people who were arrogant said: “We shall surely drive you out of our town, O Shuʿayb, you and those who have believed with you, unless you return to our religion.” He said: “What, even though we loathe it?
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: “even though it is loathsome to us?” — one does not return from light to darkness by compulsion; faith once tasted makes the old belief hateful. To force the believer to recant is to bid him vomit up that which nourishes him.

mots.sens: we shall surely drive you out

versets.ar: قَدِ افْتَرَيْنَا عَلَى اللَّهِ كَذِبًا إِنْ عُدْنَا فِي مِلَّتِكُم بَعْدَ إِذْ نَجَّانَا اللَّهُ مِنْهَا ۚ وَمَا يَكُونُ لَنَا أَن نَّعُودَ فِيهَا إِلَّا أَن يَشَاءَ اللَّهُ رَبُّنَا ۚ وَسِعَ رَبُّنَا كُلَّ شَيْءٍ عِلْمًا ۚ عَلَى اللَّهِ تَوَكَّلْنَا ۚ رَبَّنَا افْتَحْ بَيْنَنَا وَبَيْنَ قَوْمِنَا بِالْحَقِّ وَأَنتَ خَيْرُ الْفَاتِحِينَ
versets.tr: qadi ftaraynā ʿalā llāhi kadhiban in ʿudnā fī millatikum baʿda idh najjānā llāhu minhā wa-mā yakūnu lanā an naʿūda fīhā illā an yashāʾa llāhu rabbunā wasiʿa rabbunā kulla shayʾin ʿilman ʿalā llāhi tawakkalnā rabbanā-ftaḥ baynanā wa-bayna qawminā bi-l-ḥaqqi wa-anta khayru l-fātiḥīn
versets.trad: We would be forging a lie against God if we returned to your religion after God has delivered us from it. It is not for us to return to it unless God, our Lord, should so will. Our Lord’s knowledge encompasses all things. In God we place our trust. Our Lord! Decide between us and our people in truth, for You are the best of those who decide!”
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: “upon God we rely” — tawakkul is to lay one’s fate in the hands of the One whose knowledge embraces all. Shuʿayb neither threatens nor implores his enemies: he surrenders, and entrusts the verdict to the best of judges. The heart’s repose lies in this abandonment.

versets.ar: وَقَالَ الْمَلَأُ الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا مِن قَوْمِهِ لَئِنِ اتَّبَعْتُمْ شُعَيْبًا إِنَّكُمْ إِذًا لَّخَاسِرُونَ
versets.tr: wa-qāla l-malaʾu lladhīna kafarū min qawmihi la-ʾini ttabaʿtum shuʿayban innakum idhan la-khāsirūn
versets.trad: And the notables of his people who disbelieved said: “If you follow Shuʿayb, you will surely be the losers!”
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: they invert the truth: they name salvation loss and ruin gain. Such is the speech of overturned hearts; what they proclaim as ruin is precisely deliverance, and their prophecy of misfortune will recoil upon themselves.

versets.ar: فَأَخَذَتْهُمُ الرَّجْفَةُ فَأَصْبَحُوا فِي دَارِهِمْ جَاثِمِينَ
versets.tr: fa-akhadhathumu r-rajfatu fa-aṣbaḥū fī dārihim jāthimīn
versets.trad: Then the earthquake seized them, and the morning found them lying lifeless in their dwellings.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: the same convulsion that annihilated Thamūd strikes Madyan; the names of the peoples change but the law is one — whoever defies the Real after the proof reaps the same dawn of ashes.

versets.ar: الَّذِينَ كَذَّبُوا شُعَيْبًا كَأَن لَّمْ يَغْنَوْا فِيهَا ۚ الَّذِينَ كَذَّبُوا شُعَيْبًا كَانُوا هُمُ الْخَاسِرِينَ
versets.tr: alladhīna kadhdhabū shuʿayban ka-an lam yaghnaw fīhā lladhīna kadhdhabū shuʿayban kānū humu l-khāsirīn
versets.trad: Those who had called Shuʿayb a liar vanished as though they had never dwelt there. Those who had called Shuʿayb a liar, it was they who were the losers.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: “as though they had never dwelt there” — all their prosperity is wiped away like a trace upon the sand; and those who foretold loss for the believers discover themselves to be the only losers. The threat they brandished has folded back upon their own heads.

mots.sens: as though they had never dwelt
mots.def: as though they had never prospered.

versets.ar: فَتَوَلَّىٰ عَنْهُمْ وَقَالَ يَا قَوْمِ لَقَدْ أَبْلَغْتُكُمْ رِسَالَاتِ رَبِّي وَنَصَحْتُ لَكُمْ ۖ فَكَيْفَ آسَىٰ عَلَىٰ قَوْمٍ كَافِرِينَ
versets.tr: fa-tawallā ʿanhum wa-qāla yā qawmi la-qad ablaghtukum risālāti rabbī wa-naṣaḥtu lakum fa-kayfa āsā ʿalā qawmin kāfirīn
versets.trad: So he turned away from them and said: “O my people! I have indeed conveyed to you the messages of my Lord and given you sincere counsel. How then should I grieve over a disbelieving people?”
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: “how should I grieve?” — when the prophet has given everything, sorrow ceases; to grieve for the wilful disbeliever would be to set oneself against the decree. The sage’s heart weeps so long as it hopes, and grows still when the door of return has been shut by their own choice.

versets.ar: وَمَا أَرْسَلْنَا فِي قَرْيَةٍ مِّن نَّبِيٍّ إِلَّا أَخَذْنَا أَهْلَهَا بِالْبَأْسَاءِ وَالضَّرَّاءِ لَعَلَّهُمْ يَضَّرَّعُونَ
versets.tr: wa-mā arsalnā fī qaryatin min nabiyyin illā akhadhnā ahlahā bi-l-baʾsāʾi wa-ḍ-ḍarrāʾi laʿallahum yaḍḍarraʿūn
versets.trad: We sent no prophet into any town but that We seized its people with hardship and affliction, that perhaps they might humble themselves.
themes: the trial that turns one back to God
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: the trial is mercy in disguise: God sends affliction to break the hardness of the heart and bring it back to humility (taḍarruʿ). The calamity that bends the proud toward prayer is better than the ease that lulls him into forgetfulness.

versets.ar: ثُمَّ بَدَّلْنَا مَكَانَ السَّيِّئَةِ الْحَسَنَةَ حَتَّىٰ عَفَوا وَّقَالُوا قَدْ مَسَّ آبَاءَنَا الضَّرَّاءُ وَالسَّرَّاءُ فَأَخَذْنَاهُم بَغْتَةً وَهُمْ لَا يَشْعُرُونَ
versets.tr: thumma baddalnā makāna s-sayyiʾati l-ḥasanata ḥattā ʿafaw wa-qālū qad massa ābāʾanā ḍ-ḍarrāʾu wa-s-sarrāʾu fa-akhadhnāhum baghtatan wa-hum lā yashʿurūn
versets.trad: Then We replaced the evil with good, until they flourished and forgot, saying: “Hardship and ease touched our fathers before us.” So We seized them suddenly, when they were unaware.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: more to be feared than the trial is the ease that follows it: it dulls vigilance and makes one forget the Giver. They ascribed to the course of things what came from God, and the seizure took them in their heedlessness. Hardship awakens; a blessing ill received lulls one to the very edge of the abyss.

versets.ar: وَلَوْ أَنَّ أَهْلَ الْقُرَىٰ آمَنُوا وَاتَّقَوْا لَفَتَحْنَا عَلَيْهِم بَرَكَاتٍ مِّنَ السَّمَاءِ وَالْأَرْضِ وَلَٰكِن كَذَّبُوا فَأَخَذْنَاهُم بِمَا كَانُوا يَكْسِبُونَ
versets.tr: wa-law anna ahla l-qurā āmanū wa-ttaqaw la-fataḥnā ʿalayhim barakātin mina s-samāʾi wa-l-arḍi wa-lākin kadhdhabū fa-akhadhnāhum bimā kānū yaksibūn
versets.trad: Had the people of those towns believed and been mindful of God, We would have opened to them blessings from heaven and earth; but they cried lies, so We seized them for what they had earned.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: faith and godly fear open the gates of blessing from above and below; heaven and earth are miserly toward the denier and lavish toward the believer. The barakah does not descend upon the closed heart, for the gift follows the disposition to receive it.

mots.racine: س م و / أ ر ض

versets.ar: أَفَأَمِنَ أَهْلُ الْقُرَىٰ أَن يَأْتِيَهُم بَأْسُنَا بَيَاتًا وَهُمْ نَائِمُونَ
versets.tr: a-fa-amina ahlu l-qurā an yaʾtiyahum baʾsunā bayātan wa-hum nāʾimūn
versets.trad: Did the people of the towns feel secure that Our might would not come upon them by night, while they slept?
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: the slumber of the heedless makes him think himself safe; but the decree keeps watch while the negligent sleeps. None is secure against the might so long as he lives in forgetfulness, were it in the depths of his repose.

versets.ar: أَوَأَمِنَ أَهْلُ الْقُرَىٰ أَن يَأْتِيَهُم بَأْسُنَا ضُحًى وَهُمْ يَلْعَبُونَ
versets.tr: a-wa-amina ahlu l-qurā an yaʾtiyahum baʾsunā ḍuḥan wa-hum yalʿabūn
versets.trad: Or did the people of the towns feel secure that Our might would not come upon them in broad morning, while they played?
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: by night in sleep, by day in play — the heedless is never without diversion. But the morning of play may be the morning of chastisement. The heart’s vigilance knows neither hour of rest nor hour of amusement.

versets.ar: أَفَأَمِنُوا مَكْرَ اللَّهِ ۚ فَلَا يَأْمَنُ مَكْرَ اللَّهِ إِلَّا الْقَوْمُ الْخَاسِرُونَ
versets.tr: a-fa-aminū makra llāhi fa-lā yaʾmanu makra llāhi illā l-qawmu l-khāsirūn
versets.trad: Did they then feel secure from the design of God? None feels secure from the design of God but a people who are lost.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: to feel safe from the design of God (makr Allāh) is the mark of the lost; the true servant lives between fear and hope, leaning neither on his works nor on his reprieve. The divine “stratagem” is to heap upon the forgetful blessings that ruin him gently.

versets.ar: أَوَلَمْ يَهْدِ لِلَّذِينَ يَرِثُونَ الْأَرْضَ مِن بَعْدِ أَهْلِهَا أَن لَّوْ نَشَاءُ أَصَبْنَاهُم بِذُنُوبِهِمْ ۚ وَنَطْبَعُ عَلَىٰ قُلُوبِهِمْ فَهُمْ لَا يَسْمَعُونَ
versets.tr: a-wa-lam yahdi li-lladhīna yarithūna l-arḍa min baʿdi ahlihā an law nashāʾu aṣabnāhum bi-dhunūbihim wa-naṭbaʿu ʿalā qulūbihim fa-hum lā yasmaʿūn
versets.trad: Is it not made plain to those who inherit the earth after its former dwellers that, if We willed, We could strike them for their sins? And We set a seal upon their hearts, so that they no longer hear.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: the ruins of the vanished are a guidance offered to the heirs; but the seal set upon the hearts closes the ear. The worst chastisement is not the outward blow but this inward seal that renders one deaf to the call — a punishment fed by sin itself.

versets.ar: تِلْكَ الْقُرَىٰ نَقُصُّ عَلَيْكَ مِنْ أَنبَائِهَا ۚ وَلَقَدْ جَاءَتْهُمْ رُسُلُهُم بِالْبَيِّنَاتِ فَمَا كَانُوا لِيُؤْمِنُوا بِمَا كَذَّبُوا مِن قَبْلُ ۚ كَذَٰلِكَ يَطْبَعُ اللَّهُ عَلَىٰ قُلُوبِ الْكَافِرِينَ
