versets.tr: tilka l-qurā naquṣṣu ʿalayka min anbāʾihā wa-la-qad jāʾathum rusuluhum bi-l-bayyināti fa-mā kānū li-yuʾminū bimā kadhdhabū min qablu kadhālika yaṭbaʿu llāhu ʿalā qulūbi l-kāfirīn
versets.trad: Such are the towns whose tidings We relate to you. Their messengers came to them with clear proofs, yet they could not believe in what they had earlier denied. Thus does God set a seal upon the hearts of the disbelievers.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: repeated denial hardens the heart until it can no longer believe; the first refusal becomes, little by little, an impossibility. Thus free sin begets bondage: by dint of closing the door, one loses the strength to open it.

versets.ar: وَمَا وَجَدْنَا لِأَكْثَرِهِم مِّنْ عَهْدٍ ۖ وَإِن وَجَدْنَا أَكْثَرَهُمْ لَفَاسِقِينَ
versets.tr: wa-mā wajadnā li-aktharihim min ʿahdin wa-in wajadnā aktharahum la-fāsiqīn
versets.trad: And in most of them We found no fidelity to the covenant; rather, We found most of them to be transgressors.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: a foreshadowing of the great theme to come: most have forgotten the primordial covenant. The Pact was sealed in every soul, but rare are those who keep faith with it; unfaithfulness to the ʿahd is the forgetting of that “yes” pronounced before time.

versets.ar: ثُمَّ بَعَثْنَا مِن بَعْدِهِم مُّوسَىٰ بِآيَاتِنَا إِلَىٰ فِرْعَوْنَ وَمَلَئِهِ فَظَلَمُوا بِهَا ۖ فَانظُرْ كَيْفَ كَانَ عَاقِبَةُ الْمُفْسِدِينَ
versets.tr: thumma baʿathnā min baʿdihim mūsā bi-āyātinā ilā firʿawna wa-malaʾihi fa-ẓalamū bihā fa-nẓur kayfa kāna ʿāqibatu l-mufsidīn
versets.trad: Then, after them, We sent Mūsā with Our signs to Pharaoh and his notables, but they dealt unjustly with them. See, then, what was the end of those who spread corruption!
versets.commentaire: Here opens the long cycle of Mūsā, the narrative summit of the sūra. Qushayrī: Mūsā was sent bearing dazzling signs, but the heart of Pharaoh was sealed by his claim to divinity; no sign pierces the veil of one who has made himself a god in his own eyes.

versets.ar: وَقَالَ مُوسَىٰ يَا فِرْعَوْنُ إِنِّي رَسُولٌ مِّن رَّبِّ الْعَالَمِينَ
versets.tr: wa-qāla mūsā yā firʿawnu innī rasūlun min rabbi l-ʿālamīn
versets.trad: Mūsā said: “O Pharaoh! Truly I am a messenger from the Lord of the worlds.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: Mūsā stands before the tyrant without fear, strong in his commission alone; to Pharaoh’s “I am your lord most high” he opposes “I am the messenger of the Lord of the worlds.” To the creature’s pretension he answers with effacement before the Real.

mots.racine: ر ب ب / ع ل م

versets.ar: حَقِيقٌ عَلَىٰ أَن لَّا أَقُولَ عَلَى اللَّهِ إِلَّا الْحَقَّ ۚ قَدْ جِئْتُكُم بِبَيِّنَةٍ مِّن رَّبِّكُمْ فَأَرْسِلْ مَعِيَ بَنِي إِسْرَائِيلَ
versets.tr: ḥaqīqun ʿalā an lā aqūla ʿalā llāhi illā l-ḥaqqa qad jiʾtukum bi-bayyinatin min rabbikum fa-arsil maʿiya banī isrāʾīl
versets.trad: It is binding upon me to say nothing of God but the truth. I have come to you with a clear proof from your Lord. So send with me the Children of Israel!”
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: “I say nothing of God but the truth” — the prophet is bound by truthfulness as by an oath; he mixes nothing of himself into the message. And he demands the freedom of the oppressed, for the tawḥīd he bears also delivers from the tyrant’s chains.

versets.ar: قَالَ إِن كُنتَ جِئْتَ بِآيَةٍ فَأْتِ بِهَا إِن كُنتَ مِنَ الصَّادِقِينَ
versets.tr: qāla in kunta jiʾta bi-āyatin fa-ʾti bihā in kunta mina ṣ-ṣādiqīn
versets.trad: Pharaoh said: “If you have come with a sign, then produce it, if you are among the truthful.”
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: Pharaoh demands a sign, not to believe but to defy; the sealed heart asks for proofs it will never accept, for what it lacks is not the proof but the will to submit.

versets.ar: فَأَلْقَىٰ عَصَاهُ فَإِذَا هِيَ ثُعْبَانٌ مُّبِينٌ
versets.tr: fa-alqā ʿaṣāhu fa-idhā hiya thuʿbānun mubīn
versets.trad: So he cast down his staff, and behold, it was a serpent plain to see.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: the staff, mere dead wood, becomes a living serpent at the command: a sign that life and death, form and its transformation, are in the hands of God alone. What seems inert quickens at His word.

versets.ar: وَنَزَعَ يَدَهُ فَإِذَا هِيَ بَيْضَاءُ لِلنَّاظِرِينَ
versets.tr: wa-nazaʿa yadahu fa-idhā hiya bayḍāʾu li-n-nāẓirīn
versets.trad: Then he drew forth his hand, and behold, it was white to the beholders.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: the hand that comes forth radiant with light is the sign of knowledge that shines; after the serpent that terrifies, the whiteness that reassures — rigour and gentleness, two faces of the same divine unveiling.

versets.ar: قَالَ الْمَلَأُ مِن قَوْمِ فِرْعَوْنَ إِنَّ هَٰذَا لَسَاحِرٌ عَلِيمٌ
versets.tr: qāla l-malaʾu min qawmi firʿawna inna hādhā la-sāḥirun ʿalīm
versets.trad: The notables of Pharaoh’s people exclaimed: “This one is indeed a learned sorcerer.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: unable to deny the wonder, they disguise it as sorcery; unable to put out the light, they falsify its name. To call the miracle magic is the last ruse of one who sees without willing to acknowledge.

versets.ar: يُرِيدُ أَن يُخْرِجَكُم مِّنْ أَرْضِكُمْ ۖ فَمَاذَا تَأْمُرُونَ
versets.tr: yurīdu an yukhrijakum min arḍikum fa-mādhā taʾmurūn
versets.trad: He means to drive you out of your land. What, then, do you advise?”
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: they clothe their fear of losing power in a show of concern for the homeland; the tyrant and his notables fear not for the people but for their throne. The truth always threatens first those who reign by falsehood.

versets.ar: قَالُوا أَرْجِهْ وَأَخَاهُ وَأَرْسِلْ فِي الْمَدَائِنِ حَاشِرِينَ
versets.tr: qālū arjih wa-akhāhu wa-arsil fī l-madāʾini ḥāshirīn
versets.trad: They said [to Pharaoh]: “Put him off, him and his brother, and send heralds throughout the cities to gather
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: they seek to pit their art against the miracle, believing truth reducible to a skill that one outdoes by another; but no artifice stands before the authentic sign. They muster their whole world against a single man leaning upon his Lord.

versets.ar: يَأْتُوكَ بِكُلِّ سَاحِرٍ عَلِيمٍ
versets.tr: yaʾtūka bi-kulli sāḥirin ʿalīm
versets.trad: every learned sorcerer and bring them to you.”
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: they summon all the knowledge of men to confront the knowledge of God; but what comes from Him is not opposed, it is revealed. The gathering of every sorcerer will only magnify the defeat and exalt the sign.

mots.racine: س ح ر / ع ل م

versets.ar: وَجَاءَ السَّحَرَةُ فِرْعَوْنَ قَالُوا إِنَّ لَنَا لَأَجْرًا إِن كُنَّا نَحْنُ الْغَالِبِينَ
versets.tr: wa-jāʾa s-saḥaratu firʿawna qālū inna lanā la-ajran in kunnā naḥnu l-ghālibīn
versets.trad: The sorcerers came to Pharaoh and said: “Shall we surely have a reward, if it is we who prevail?”
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: the sorcerers bargain their art for a wage; they serve for the reward, not for the truth. What a contrast with what they will become: mercenaries one moment, martyrs the next, having given all for God.

versets.ar: قَالَ نَعَمْ وَإِنَّكُمْ لَمِنَ الْمُقَرَّبِينَ
versets.tr: qāla naʿam wa-innakum la-mina l-muqarrabīn
versets.trad: He said: “Yes, and you shall then be among those brought near.”
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: Pharaoh promises nearness to his throne — the illusory nearness of a mortal king. Soon the sorcerers will disdain this favour for the true nearness, that of the Lord of the worlds, exchanging the court of the tyrant for the court of God.

versets.ar: قَالُوا يَا مُوسَىٰ إِمَّا أَن تُلْقِيَ وَإِمَّا أَن نَّكُونَ نَحْنُ الْمُلْقِينَ
versets.tr: qālū yā mūsā immā an tulqiya wa-immā an nakūna naḥnu l-mulqīn
versets.trad: They said: “O Mūsā! Will you cast, or shall we be the ones to cast?”
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: they offer a choice, thinking the matter a duel of equals; but Mūsā, sure of his Lord, leaves them the first move — for the Real need not fear the opening stroke of the false, knowing that the outcome belongs to Him.

versets.ar: قَالَ أَلْقُوا ۖ فَلَمَّا أَلْقَوْا سَحَرُوا أَعْيُنَ النَّاسِ وَاسْتَرْهَبُوهُمْ وَجَاءُوا بِسِحْرٍ عَظِيمٍ
versets.tr: qāla alqū fa-lammā alqaw saḥarū aʿyuna n-nāsi wa-starhabūhum wa-jāʾū bi-siḥrin ʿaẓīm
versets.trad: He said: “Cast!” And when they had cast, they bewitched the eyes of the people, struck them with terror, and produced a mighty sorcery.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: “they bewitched the eyes of the people” — sorcery works only upon the sight, not upon the real; it changes the appearance without touching the being. Such is every illusion: it terrifies the eye but can create nothing. The false impresses; only the true transforms.

mots.racine: س ح ر / ع ي ن

versets.ar: وَأَوْحَيْنَا إِلَىٰ مُوسَىٰ أَنْ أَلْقِ عَصَاكَ ۖ فَإِذَا هِيَ تَلْقَفُ مَا يَأْفِكُونَ
versets.tr: wa-awḥaynā ilā mūsā an alqi ʿaṣāka fa-idhā hiya talqafu mā yaʾfikūn
versets.trad: Then We inspired Mūsā: “Cast your staff!” And behold, it swallowed up all that they had falsely contrived.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: the staff of truth swallows in an instant all their artifice; when the Real appears, the illusion vanishes like a shadow before the sun. A single authentic sign sweeps away a thousand deceiving wonders, for the false has no subsistence of its own.

versets.ar: فَوَقَعَ الْحَقُّ وَبَطَلَ مَا كَانُوا يَعْمَلُونَ
versets.tr: fa-waqaʿa l-ḥaqqu wa-baṭala mā kānū yaʿmalūn
versets.trad: So the Truth prevailed, and all that they had wrought came to nothing.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: “the Truth prevailed and their work was brought to nought” — when the Real arises, the false falls of itself; it needs no battle, its mere presence suffices. The night does not contend with the dawn: it simply fades away.

mots.racine: و ق ع / ح ق ق
mots.def: the Real manifested itself and triumphed.

versets.ar: فَغُلِبُوا هُنَالِكَ وَانقَلَبُوا صَاغِرِينَ
versets.tr: fa-ghulibū hunālika wa-nqalabū ṣāghirīn
versets.trad: So they were defeated there and turned back humbled.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: those who sought the humiliation of Mūsā turn back humbled; the snare they set springs shut upon its setters. The arrogance that defies the Real is always overturned into abasement.

versets.ar: وَأُلْقِيَ السَّحَرَةُ سَاجِدِينَ
versets.tr: wa-ulqiya s-saḥaratu sājidīn
versets.trad: And the sorcerers were cast down in prostration,
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: “they were cast down in prostration” — not that they prostrate of themselves, but that a force lays them low; the truth, when it seizes, throws one down by force to the ground. Those who knew sorcery best were the first to recognize what was no sorcery: their very knowledge led them to faith.

mots.def: thrown down as if despite themselves.

versets.ar: قَالُوا آمَنَّا بِرَبِّ الْعَالَمِينَ
versets.tr: qālū āmannā bi-rabbi l-ʿālamīn
versets.trad: and cried out: “We believe in the Lord of the worlds,
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: in a single instant the hireling becomes a believer; true faith does not advance by degrees but falls like lightning upon the heart made ready. Those who had come for a wage depart with the highest reward.

mots.racine: ر ب ب / ع ل م

versets.ar: رَبِّ مُوسَىٰ وَهَارُونَ
versets.tr: rabbi mūsā wa-hārūn
versets.trad: the Lord of Moses and Aaron!”
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: they specify “the Lord of Mūsā and Hārūn” to rule out any confusion with Pharaoh, who claimed to be a lord; their faith is lucid, naming its object without ambiguity. True knowledge distinguishes the real Lord from the idol that usurps His name.

versets.ar: قَالَ فِرْعَوْنُ آمَنتُم بِهِ قَبْلَ أَنْ آذَنَ لَكُمْ ۖ إِنَّ هَٰذَا لَمَكْرٌ مَّكَرْتُمُوهُ فِي الْمَدِينَةِ لِتُخْرِجُوا مِنْهَا أَهْلَهَا ۖ فَسَوْفَ تَعْلَمُونَ
versets.tr: qāla firʿawnu āmantum bihi qabla an ādhana lakum inna hādhā la-makrun makartumūhu fī l-madīnati li-tukhrijū minhā ahlahā fa-sawfa taʿlamūn
versets.trad: Pharaoh said to them: “So you would believe in him before I grant you leave? This is surely a plot you have devised in the city to drive its people out of it. Soon you shall know,
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: Pharaoh is outraged that men should believe “without his leave” — as though faith depended on the permission of a mortal. Such is the delirium of the tyrant: he imagines himself master of hearts. But faith is a gift of God that asks the consent of no one.

versets.ar: لَأُقَطِّعَنَّ أَيْدِيَكُمْ وَأَرْجُلَكُم مِّنْ خِلَافٍ ثُمَّ لَأُصَلِّبَنَّكُمْ أَجْمَعِينَ
versets.tr: la-uqaṭṭiʿanna aydiyakum wa-arjulakum min khilāfin thumma la-uṣallibannakum ajmaʿīn
versets.trad: I will surely cut off your hands and your feet on opposite sides, then I will surely crucify you all.”
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: the tyrant knows no answer to faith but the torture of the body, unaware that he cannot reach the heart; he threatens the limbs while the soul escapes him. The torment he brandishes will become for the believers the gate of martyrdom, not of defeat.

versets.ar: قَالُوا إِنَّا إِلَىٰ رَبِّنَا مُنقَلِبُونَ
versets.tr: qālū innā ilā rabbinā munqalibūn
versets.trad: They said: “It is to our Lord that we turn back!
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: “it is to our Lord that we return” — the threat of death does not frighten them, for they know that death is but a return; one threatens with the hereafter only him who dreads it. For the one who longs for God, the torment is but a shortcut to the meeting.

versets.ar: وَمَا تَنقِمُ مِنَّا إِلَّا أَنْ آمَنَّا بِآيَاتِ رَبِّنَا لَمَّا جَاءَتْنَا ۚ رَبَّنَا أَفْرِغْ عَلَيْنَا صَبْرًا وَتَوَفَّنَا مُسْلِمِينَ
versets.tr: wa-mā tanqimu minnā illā an āmannā bi-āyāti rabbinā lammā jāʾatnā rabbanā afrigh ʿalaynā ṣabran wa-tawaffanā muslimīn
versets.trad: You take vengeance on us only because we believed in the signs of our Lord when they came to us. Our Lord! Pour upon us patience and let us die in submission!”
themes: the prayer of the new believers
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: “pour upon us patience and take us back submitted” — they ask neither for deliverance nor for vengeance, but for patience and an ending in islām. Their only fear is not the torment but to die outside of faith. Such is the instant maturity of hearts touched by the Real.

versets.ar: وَقَالَ الْمَلَأُ مِن قَوْمِ فِرْعَوْنَ أَتَذَرُ مُوسَىٰ وَقَوْمَهُ لِيُفْسِدُوا فِي الْأَرْضِ وَيَذَرَكَ وَآلِهَتَكَ ۚ قَالَ سَنُقَتِّلُ أَبْنَاءَهُمْ وَنَسْتَحْيِي نِسَاءَهُمْ وَإِنَّا فَوْقَهُمْ قَاهِرُونَ
versets.tr: wa-qāla l-malaʾu min qawmi firʿawna a-tadharu mūsā wa-qawmahu li-yufsidū fī l-arḍi wa-yadharaka wa-ālihataka qāla sa-nuqattilu abnāʾahum wa-nastaḥyī nisāʾahum wa-innā fawqahum qāhirūn
versets.trad: The notables of Pharaoh's people said: “Will you leave Moses and his people to spread corruption in the land and to forsake you and your gods?” He answered: “We shall slay their sons and spare their women. Assuredly, we hold them in our power!”
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: “we hold them in our power” — the tyrant believes he reigns by force, unaware that he is but an instrument of the decree. His cruelty against the children of Israel is only the veil behind which their deliverance is being prepared; the oppressor labours unknowingly for the victory of the oppressed.

versets.ar: قَالَ مُوسَىٰ لِقَوْمِهِ اسْتَعِينُوا بِاللَّهِ وَاصْبِرُوا ۖ إِنَّ الْأَرْضَ لِلَّهِ يُورِثُهَا مَن يَشَاءُ مِنْ عِبَادِهِ ۖ وَالْعَاقِبَةُ لِلْمُتَّقِينَ
versets.tr: qāla mūsā li-qawmihi staʿīnū bi-llāhi wa-ṣbirū inna l-arḍa li-llāhi yūrithuhā man yashāʾu min ʿibādihi wa-l-ʿāqibatu li-l-muttaqīn
versets.trad: Moses said to his people: “Seek the help of God and be patient. The earth belongs to God, and He gives it in inheritance to whom He wills among His servants. The blessed end awaits those who are mindful of Him.”
themes: the help of God and patience
the end promised to the God-fearing
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: “the help of God and patience” are the two wings of the oppressed; and “the end belongs to the God-fearing” is the promise that sustains in the trial. Mūsā offers not an immediate deliverance but an endurance inhabited by hope, for the earth returns to whoever entrusts himself to his Lord.

versets.ar: قَالُوا أُوذِينَا مِن قَبْلِ أَن تَأْتِيَنَا وَمِن بَعْدِ مَا جِئْتَنَا ۚ قَالَ عَسَىٰ رَبُّكُمْ أَن يُهْلِكَ عَدُوَّكُمْ وَيَسْتَخْلِفَكُمْ فِي الْأَرْضِ فَيَنظُرَ كَيْفَ تَعْمَلُونَ
versets.tr: qālū ūdhīnā min qabli an taʾtiyanā wa-min baʿdi mā jiʾtanā qāla ʿasā rabbukum an yuhlika ʿaduwwakum wa-yastakhlifakum fī l-arḍi fa-yanẓura kayfa taʿmalūn
versets.trad: They said: “We have suffered both before you came to us and after your coming.” He said: “Perhaps your Lord will destroy your enemy and make you successors in the land, so as to see how you will act.”
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: “so as to see how you will act” — the inheritance promised is not an end but a new trial; deliverance is itself an examination. Many hold firm in adversity and fall in power; the authority received tests more harshly than the oppression endured.

versets.ar: وَلَقَدْ أَخَذْنَا آلَ فِرْعَوْنَ بِالسِّنِينَ وَنَقْصٍ مِّنَ الثَّمَرَاتِ لَعَلَّهُمْ يَذَّكَّرُونَ
versets.tr: wa-la-qad akhadhnā āla firʿawna bi-s-sinīna wa-naqṣin mina th-thamarāti laʿallahum yadhdhakkarūn
versets.trad: We struck the people of Pharaoh with years of drought and a dearth of fruits, that perhaps they might take heed.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: famine is a reminder addressed to the hardened heart; God tightens provision in order to reopen remembrance. But the sealed heart reads misfortune as mere chance, not as a sign, and so misses the lesson.

versets.ar: فَإِذَا جَاءَتْهُمُ الْحَسَنَةُ قَالُوا لَنَا هَٰذِهِ ۖ وَإِن تُصِبْهُمْ سَيِّئَةٌ يَطَّيَّرُوا بِمُوسَىٰ وَمَن مَّعَهُ ۗ أَلَا إِنَّمَا طَائِرُهُمْ عِندَ اللَّهِ وَلَٰكِنَّ أَكْثَرَهُمْ لَا يَعْلَمُونَ
versets.tr: fa-idhā jāʾathumu l-ḥasanatu qālū lanā hādhihi wa-in tuṣibhum sayyiʾatun yaṭṭayyarū bi-mūsā wa-man maʿahu a-lā innamā ṭāʾiruhum ʿinda llāhi wa-lākinna aktharahum lā yaʿlamūn
versets.trad: When prosperity came to them they would say: “This is what we are due!” But when misfortune struck them, they laid the evil omen upon Moses and those with him. Yet their fate rests only with God, though most of them do not know it.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: they claim the good for themselves and impute the evil to others; such is the blindness of the soul — to see within itself the cause of happiness and outside itself the cause of misfortune. But “their omen is with God”: all comes from Him, and their superstition is but a flight from this truth.

versets.ar: وَقَالُوا مَهْمَا تَأْتِنَا بِهِ مِنْ آيَةٍ لِّتَسْحَرَنَا بِهَا فَمَا نَحْنُ لَكَ بِمُؤْمِنِينَ
versets.tr: wa-qālū mahmā taʾtinā bihi min āyatin li-tasḥaranā bihā fa-mā naḥnu laka bi-muʾminīn
versets.trad: They said: “Whatever sign you bring us to bewitch us with, we will not believe in you!”
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: they decree their disbelief in advance, closing the door before they even look; no sign convinces the one resolved not to believe. The will to refuse precedes the proof and renders it vain. The sealed heart opens to no miracle.

versets.ar: فَأَرْسَلْنَا عَلَيْهِمُ الطُّوفَانَ وَالْجَرَادَ وَالْقُمَّلَ وَالضَّفَادِعَ وَالدَّمَ آيَاتٍ مُّفَصَّلَاتٍ فَاسْتَكْبَرُوا وَكَانُوا قَوْمًا مُّجْرِمِينَ
versets.tr: fa-arsalnā ʿalayhimu ṭ-ṭūfāna wa-l-jarāda wa-l-qummala wa-ḍ-ḍafādiʿa wa-d-dama āyātin mufaṣṣalātin fa-stakbarū wa-kānū qawman mujrimīn
versets.trad: So We sent against them the flood, the locusts, the lice, the frogs and the blood, as distinct signs. But they swelled with pride and remained a guilty people.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: the plagues follow one upon another, “distinct signs” offered one by one to give each time a chance for repentance; but pride wipes them all away. When the heart has hardened, the multiplying of warnings only thickens the veil instead of lifting it.

versets.ar: وَلَمَّا وَقَعَ عَلَيْهِمُ الرِّجْزُ قَالُوا يَا مُوسَى ادْعُ لَنَا رَبَّكَ بِمَا عَهِدَ عِندَكَ ۖ لَئِن كَشَفْتَ عَنَّا الرِّجْزَ لَنُؤْمِنَنَّ لَكَ وَلَنُرْسِلَنَّ مَعَكَ بَنِي إِسْرَائِيلَ
versets.tr: wa-lammā waqaʿa ʿalayhimu r-rijzu qālū yā mūsā-dʿu lanā rabbaka bimā ʿahida ʿindaka la-ʾin kashafta ʿannā r-rijza la-nuʾminanna laka wa-la-nursilanna maʿaka banī isrāʾīl
versets.trad: Whenever a plague fell upon them they would say: “O Moses! Call upon your Lord for us by virtue of the covenant He has granted you. If you remove this plague from us we will believe in you and will send the children of Israel away with you.”
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: the faith promised under the constraint of the plague is not faith: it is a bargaining of fear. They swear to believe so that the pain may cease, but as soon as they are relieved they recant. True faith is born of love or of vision, not of the mere terror of the blow.

versets.ar: فَلَمَّا كَشَفْنَا عَنْهُمُ الرِّجْزَ إِلَىٰ أَجَلٍ هُم بَالِغُوهُ إِذَا هُمْ يَنكُثُونَ
versets.tr: fa-lammā kashafnā ʿanhumu r-rijza ilā ajalin hum bālighūhu idhā hum yankuthūn
versets.trad: But when We spared them the plague until a term they were to reach, they broke their pledge.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: no sooner is the plague lifted than the oath is broken; the heart that believed only out of fear falls back into denial as soon as the fear withdraws. The perjury reveals that their promise was a lie, and the lifting of the chastisement was but a last reprieve to complete their proof against themselves.

versets.ar: فَانتَقَمْنَا مِنْهُمْ فَأَغْرَقْنَاهُمْ فِي الْيَمِّ بِأَنَّهُمْ كَذَّبُوا بِآيَاتِنَا وَكَانُوا عَنْهَا غَافِلِينَ
versets.tr: fa-ntaqamnā minhum fa-aghraqnāhum fī l-yammi bi-annahum kadhdhabū bi-āyātinā wa-kānū ʿanhā ghāfilīn
versets.trad: So We took vengeance upon them; We drowned them in the sea because they denied Our signs and were heedless of them.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: the sea that engulfs them is the instrument of the decree; they perished by water, they who had drowned in forgetfulness. Heedlessness (ghafla) of the signs had submerged them within before the waves submerged them without.

mots.def: the expanse of water, the Red Sea.

versets.ar: وَأَوْرَثْنَا الْقَوْمَ الَّذِينَ كَانُوا يُسْتَضْعَفُونَ مَشَارِقَ الْأَرْضِ وَمَغَارِبَهَا الَّتِي بَارَكْنَا فِيهَا ۖ وَتَمَّتْ كَلِمَتُ رَبِّكَ الْحُسْنَىٰ عَلَىٰ بَنِي إِسْرَائِيلَ بِمَا صَبَرُوا ۖ وَدَمَّرْنَا مَا كَانَ يَصْنَعُ فِرْعَوْنُ وَقَوْمُهُ وَمَا كَانُوا يَعْرِشُونَ
versets.tr: wa-awrathnā l-qawma lladhīna kānū yustaḍʿafūna mashāriqa l-arḍi wa-maghāribahā llatī bāraknā fīhā wa-tammat kalimatu rabbika l-ḥusnā ʿalā banī isrāʾīla bimā ṣabarū wa-dammarnā mā kāna yaṣnaʿu firʿawnu wa-qawmuhu wa-mā kānū yaʿrishūn
versets.trad: And We gave in inheritance to the people who had been oppressed the eastern and western lands that We had blessed. Thus was fulfilled the fair Word of your Lord to the children of Israel, because they had been patient. And We destroyed what Pharaoh and his people had wrought and what they had built.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: “the fair Word of your Lord was fulfilled for them because they were patient” — behold the fruit of ṣabr: the promise kept. The oppressed inherit the land the tyrant believed his own, and his monuments crumble. Patience is the seed, the inheritance is the harvest.

mots.racine: ش ر ق / غ ر ب
ك ل م / ح س ن
mots.sens: the fair Word of your Lord

versets.ar: وَجَاوَزْنَا بِبَنِي إِسْرَائِيلَ الْبَحْرَ فَأَتَوْا عَلَىٰ قَوْمٍ يَعْكُفُونَ عَلَىٰ أَصْنَامٍ لَّهُمْ ۚ قَالُوا يَا مُوسَى اجْعَل لَّنَا إِلَٰهًا كَمَا لَهُمْ آلِهَةٌ ۚ قَالَ إِنَّكُمْ قَوْمٌ تَجْهَلُونَ
versets.tr: wa-jāwaznā bi-banī isrāʾīla l-baḥra fa-ataw ʿalā qawmin yaʿkufūna ʿalā aṣnāmin lahum qālū yā mūsā-jʿal lanā ilāhan kamā lahum ālihatun qāla innakum qawmun tajhalūn
versets.trad: We brought the children of Israel across the sea, and they came upon a people devoted to worshipping their idols. They said: “O Moses! Make for us a god such as their gods.” He said: “You are a people who are ignorant!
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: scarcely saved from the tyrant, they demand an idol — proof that the soul freed from the outer yoke remains captive of the inner one. The pull toward the visible and the palpable is so strong that even the miracle of the parted sea does not cure the thirst for the idol.

versets.ar: إِنَّ هَٰؤُلَاءِ مُتَبَّرٌ مَّا هُمْ فِيهِ وَبَاطِلٌ مَّا كَانُوا يَعْمَلُونَ
versets.tr: inna hāʾulāʾi mutabbarun mā hum fīhi wa-bāṭilun mā kānū yaʿmalūn
versets.trad: These people follow a way doomed to perish, and their works are vain.”
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: every worship rendered to other than God is doomed to ruin; the idol and its worshipper collapse together. What has not God for its foundation has no subsistence: it is a work built upon nothingness, returning to nothingness.

versets.ar: قَالَ أَغَيْرَ اللَّهِ أَبْغِيكُمْ إِلَٰهًا وَهُوَ فَضَّلَكُمْ عَلَى الْعَالَمِينَ
versets.tr: qāla a-ghayra llāhi abghīkum ilāhan wa-huwa faḍḍalakum ʿalā l-ʿālamīn
versets.trad: He said further: “How could I seek for you a god other than God, when it is He who has favoured you above all the worlds?”
themes: the absurdity of seeking another god
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: “to seek for you a god other than God” is the height of ingratitude: the One who favoured you above all, you would forsake for a stone. The benefit received should rivet the heart to the Benefactor, not turn it away from Him.

versets.ar: وَإِذْ أَنجَيْنَاكُم مِّنْ آلِ فِرْعَوْنَ يَسُومُونَكُمْ سُوءَ الْعَذَابِ ۖ يُقَتِّلُونَ أَبْنَاءَكُمْ وَيَسْتَحْيُونَ نِسَاءَكُمْ ۚ وَفِي ذَٰلِكُم بَلَاءٌ مِّن رَّبِّكُمْ عَظِيمٌ
versets.tr: wa-idh anjaynākum min āli firʿawna yasūmūnakum sūʾa l-ʿadhābi yuqattilūna abnāʾakum wa-yastaḥyūna nisāʾakum wa-fī dhālikum balāʾun min rabbikum ʿaẓīm
versets.trad: We delivered you from the people of Pharaoh, who inflicted upon you the worst of torments; they slew your sons and spared your women. In that was a tremendous trial from your Lord!
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: “in that was a great trial” — the oppression itself was a trial, and the deliverance is another; God tests by evil and by good alike. To recall the grace of deliverance is to summon them not to betray it by relapsing into idolatry.

mots.racine: س و أ / ع ذ ب

versets.ar: وَوَاعَدْنَا مُوسَىٰ ثَلَاثِينَ لَيْلَةً وَأَتْمَمْنَاهَا بِعَشْرٍ فَتَمَّ مِيقَاتُ رَبِّهِ أَرْبَعِينَ لَيْلَةً ۚ وَقَالَ مُوسَىٰ لِأَخِيهِ هَارُونَ اخْلُفْنِي فِي قَوْمِي وَأَصْلِحْ وَلَا تَتَّبِعْ سَبِيلَ الْمُفْسِدِينَ
versets.tr: wa-wāʿadnā mūsā thalāthīna laylatan wa-atmamnāhā bi-ʿashrin fa-tamma mīqātu rabbihi arbaʿīna laylatan wa-qāla mūsā li-akhīhi hārūna-khlufnī fī qawmī wa-aṣliḥ wa-lā tattabiʿ sabīla l-mufsidīn
versets.trad: We made an appointment with Moses for thirty nights, which We completed with ten more, so that the meeting with his Lord lasted forty nights. And Moses said to his brother Aaron: “Take my place among my people. Act rightly and do not follow the way of the corrupters.”
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: the forty nights are the retreat (khalwa) in which one prepares for the meeting; none draws near to the Word without having first withdrawn and purified himself. The number forty marks the completion of the inner disposition, the threshold where the heart becomes capable of hearing.

versets.ar: وَلَمَّا جَاءَ مُوسَىٰ لِمِيقَاتِنَا وَكَلَّمَهُ رَبُّهُ قَالَ رَبِّ أَرِنِي أَنظُرْ إِلَيْكَ ۚ قَالَ لَن تَرَانِي وَلَٰكِنِ انظُرْ إِلَى الْجَبَلِ فَإِنِ اسْتَقَرَّ مَكَانَهُ فَسَوْفَ تَرَانِي ۚ فَلَمَّا تَجَلَّىٰ رَبُّهُ لِلْجَبَلِ جَعَلَهُ دَكًّا وَخَرَّ مُوسَىٰ صَعِقًا ۚ فَلَمَّا أَفَاقَ قَالَ سُبْحَانَكَ تُبْتُ إِلَيْكَ وَأَنَا أَوَّلُ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ
versets.tr: wa-lammā jāʾa mūsā li-mīqātinā wa-kallamahu rabbuhu qāla rabbi arinī anẓur ilayka qāla lan tarānī wa-lākini-nẓur ilā l-jabali fa-ini staqarra makānahu fa-sawfa tarānī fa-lammā tajallā rabbuhu li-l-jabali jaʿalahu dakkan wa-kharra mūsā ṣaʿiqan fa-lammā afāqa qāla subḥānaka tubtu ilayka wa-anā awwalu l-muʾminīn
versets.trad: When Moses came to Our meeting and his Lord spoke to him, he said: “My Lord! Show Yourself to me, that I may behold You!” The Lord said: “You shall not see Me; but look toward the mountain — if it remains in its place, then you shall see Me.” But when his Lord manifested Himself upon the mountain, He levelled it to dust, and Moses fell down in a swoon. When he came to himself, he cried: “Glory be to You! I have turned back to You in repentance, and I am the first of the believers!”
themes: the request for the vision (lan tarānī)
versets.commentaire: The mystical summit of the sura. Qushayrī: “you shall not see Me” (lan tarānī) is not an absolute refusal but the avowal of the creature's incapacity: the mountain, firmer than man, could not bear even a reflection of the manifestation and was pulverized. The swoon of Mūsā is itself a vision: he saw in losing his sight, and found in losing himself. His return to consciousness is a return to God — “I have turned back to You” — whereby the request for the vision turns into repentance and first faith.

mots.racine: ر أ ي / ن ظ ر
mots.sens: show Yourself to me that I may behold You

mots.def: negation of the vision in this world.

versets.ar: قَالَ يَا مُوسَىٰ إِنِّي اصْطَفَيْتُكَ عَلَى النَّاسِ بِرِسَالَاتِي وَبِكَلَامِي فَخُذْ مَا آتَيْتُكَ وَكُن مِّنَ الشَّاكِرِينَ
versets.tr: qāla yā mūsā innī ṣṭafaytuka ʿalā n-nāsi bi-risālātī wa-bi-kalāmī fa-khudh mā ātaytuka wa-kun mina sh-shākirīn
versets.trad: The Lord said: “O Moses! I have chosen you above all men for My messages and My speech. So take what I have given you and be among the thankful!”
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: “I have chosen you... be thankful” — the answer to lan tarānī is not abandonment but election: God consoles for the vision refused by the gift of speech and of choosing. To the one who cannot see Him, He grants to hear Him; and gratitude is the fitting response to what one receives in place of what one desired.

versets.ar: وَكَتَبْنَا لَهُ فِي الْأَلْوَاحِ مِن كُلِّ شَيْءٍ مَّوْعِظَةً وَتَفْصِيلًا لِّكُلِّ شَيْءٍ فَخُذْهَا بِقُوَّةٍ وَأْمُرْ قَوْمَكَ يَأْخُذُوا بِأَحْسَنِهَا ۚ سَأُرِيكُمْ دَارَ الْفَاسِقِينَ
versets.tr: wa-katabnā lahu fī l-alwāḥi min kulli shayʾin mawʿiẓatan wa-tafṣīlan li-kulli shayʾin fa-khudhhā bi-quwwatin wa-ʾmur qawmaka yaʾkhudhū bi-aḥsanihā sa-urīkum dāra l-fāsiqīn
versets.trad: We wrote for him upon the Tablets an exhortation on all things and an explanation in detail of everything. “So take hold of them with firmness and command your people to hold to the best of what they contain. Soon I shall show you the abode of the corrupt.”
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: “take hold of them with strength” — the Law is to be received with resolve, not with slackness; firmness (quwwa) in observance is the mark of seriousness. And “to take the best” is the invitation to aim ever at the highest of what is offered, not at the minimum permitted.

versets.ar: سَأَصْرِفُ عَنْ آيَاتِيَ الَّذِينَ يَتَكَبَّرُونَ فِي الْأَرْضِ بِغَيْرِ الْحَقِّ وَإِن يَرَوْا كُلَّ آيَةٍ لَّا يُؤْمِنُوا بِهَا وَإِن يَرَوْا سَبِيلَ الرُّشْدِ لَا يَتَّخِذُوهُ سَبِيلًا وَإِن يَرَوْا سَبِيلَ الْغَيِّ يَتَّخِذُوهُ سَبِيلًا ۚ ذَٰلِكَ بِأَنَّهُمْ كَذَّبُوا بِآيَاتِنَا وَكَانُوا عَنْهَا غَافِلِينَ
versets.tr: sa-aṣrifu ʿan āyātiya lladhīna yatakabbarūna fī l-arḍi bi-ghayri l-ḥaqqi wa-in yaraw kulla āyatin lā yuʾminū bihā wa-in yaraw sabīla r-rushdi lā yattakhidhūhu sabīlan wa-in yaraw sabīla l-ghayyi yattakhidhūhu sabīlan dhālika bi-annahum kadhdhabū bi-āyātinā wa-kānū ʿanhā ghāfilīn
versets.trad: I shall turn away from My signs those who are arrogant upon the earth without right, who, if they see any sign, do not believe in it; who, if they see the path of rectitude, do not take it as a way; and who, if they see the path of error, take it as a way. That is because they deny Our signs and remain heedless of them.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: arrogance perverts even sight: the arrogant man sees the straight path and does not take it, sees error and rushes into it. The veil of pride does not abolish vision but reverses its use; it renders one incapable of choosing the good one nonetheless sees.

mots.racine: س ب ل / ر ش د
س ب ل / غ و ي

versets.ar: وَالَّذِينَ كَذَّبُوا بِآيَاتِنَا وَلِقَاءِ الْآخِرَةِ حَبِطَتْ أَعْمَالُهُمْ ۚ هَلْ يُجْزَوْنَ إِلَّا مَا كَانُوا يَعْمَلُونَ
versets.tr: wa-lladhīna kadhdhabū bi-āyātinā wa-liqāʾi l-ākhirati ḥabiṭat aʿmāluhum hal yujzawna illā mā kānū yaʿmalūn
versets.trad: Those who denied Our signs and the meeting of the life to come — vain are their works. Shall they be requited otherwise than for what they did?
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: works deprived of faith are flowers without roots: they shine for a moment, then wither (ḥabiṭat). Without the awaited meeting, the act loses its orient and its meaning; one is requited only according to the truth of what one truly intended.

versets.ar: وَاتَّخَذَ قَوْمُ مُوسَىٰ مِن بَعْدِهِ مِنْ حُلِيِّهِمْ عِجْلًا جَسَدًا لَّهُ خُوَارٌ ۚ أَلَمْ يَرَوْا أَنَّهُ لَا يُكَلِّمُهُمْ وَلَا يَهْدِيهِمْ سَبِيلًا ۘ اتَّخَذُوهُ وَكَانُوا ظَالِمِينَ
versets.tr: wa-ttakhadha qawmu mūsā min baʿdihi min ḥuliyyihim ʿijlan jasadan lahu khuwārun a-lam yaraw annahu lā yukallimuhum wa-lā yahdīhim sabīlan ittakhadhūhu wa-kānū ẓālimīn
versets.trad: In the absence of Moses, the people made for worship, out of their ornaments, the body of a calf that lowed. Did they not see that it did not speak to them, nor guide them upon any path? They took it as a god and became wrongdoers.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: what derision: they forsake the One who spoke to Mūsā for a calf that neither speaks nor guides. The idol “lows” but says nothing; it mimics life without giving it. To worship what does not answer is blindness itself: to prefer the hollow noise to the living Word.

versets.ar: وَلَمَّا سُقِطَ فِي أَيْدِيهِمْ وَرَأَوْا أَنَّهُمْ قَدْ ضَلُّوا قَالُوا لَئِن لَّمْ يَرْحَمْنَا رَبُّنَا وَيَغْفِرْ لَنَا لَنَكُونَنَّ مِنَ الْخَاسِرِينَ
versets.tr: wa-lammā suqiṭa fī aydīhim wa-raʾaw annahum qad ḍallū qālū la-ʾin lam yarḥamnā rabbunā wa-yaghfir lanā la-nakūnanna mina l-khāsirīn
versets.trad: When they were stricken with remorse and saw that they had gone astray, they cried: “If our Lord does not have mercy on us and forgive us, we shall surely be among the lost!”
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: “to fall into their own hands” is the expression of the remorse that gnaws at itself; when the veil of error is torn away, the heart bites itself. But their cry toward mercy is already a return: the awareness of loss is the first step out of it.

mots.racine: س ق ط / ي د ي

versets.ar: وَلَمَّا رَجَعَ مُوسَىٰ إِلَىٰ قَوْمِهِ غَضْبَانَ أَسِفًا قَالَ بِئْسَمَا خَلَفْتُمُونِي مِن بَعْدِي ۖ أَعَجِلْتُمْ أَمْرَ رَبِّكُمْ ۖ وَأَلْقَى الْأَلْوَاحَ وَأَخَذَ بِرَأْسِ أَخِيهِ يَجُرُّهُ إِلَيْهِ ۚ قَالَ ابْنَ أُمَّ إِنَّ الْقَوْمَ اسْتَضْعَفُونِي وَكَادُوا يَقْتُلُونَنِي فَلَا تُشْمِتْ بِيَ الْأَعْدَاءَ وَلَا تَجْعَلْنِي مَعَ الْقَوْمِ الظَّالِمِينَ
versets.tr: wa-lammā rajaʿa mūsā ilā qawmihi ghaḍbāna asifan qāla biʾsamā khalaftumūnī min baʿdī a-ʿajiltum amra rabbikum wa-alqā l-alwāḥa wa-akhadha bi-raʾsi akhīhi yajurruhu ilayhi qāla bna umma inna l-qawma staḍʿafūnī wa-kādū yaqtulūnanī fa-lā tushmit biya l-aʿdāʾa wa-lā tajʿalnī maʿa l-qawmi ẓ-ẓālimīn
versets.trad: When Moses returned to his people, full of wrath and grief, he cried: “How vile is that which you have done in my absence! Would you hasten the command of your Lord?” He cast down the Tablets and seized his brother by the head, dragging him toward himself. Aaron said: “O son of my mother! These people overpowered me and nearly killed me. Do not let my enemies rejoice over me, and do not count me among the wrongdoers!”
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: the wrath of Mūsā is a wrath for God, not for himself; the zeal of one jealous for the divine unity flares at the sight of the idol. But Hārūn invokes “son of my mother” to awaken tenderness beneath the fury — a reminder that zeal must not crush justice toward the innocent.

mots.racine: غ ض ب / أ س ف

versets.ar: قَالَ رَبِّ اغْفِرْ لِي وَلِأَخِي وَأَدْخِلْنَا فِي رَحْمَتِكَ ۖ وَأَنتَ أَرْحَمُ الرَّاحِمِينَ
versets.tr: qāla rabbi-ghfir lī wa-li-akhī wa-adkhilnā fī raḥmatika wa-anta arḥamu r-rāḥimīn
versets.trad: Moses said: “My Lord! Forgive me and my brother, and admit us into Your mercy. You are the most merciful of the merciful.”
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: wrath appeased turns into prayer; Mūsā, understanding the innocence of Hārūn, asks forgiveness for them both. “The most merciful of the merciful” is the invocation that opens the widest door: to take refuge not in one's merit but in the immensity of His raḥma.

mots.sens: the most merciful of the merciful

versets.ar: إِنَّ الَّذِينَ اتَّخَذُوا الْعِجْلَ سَيَنَالُهُمْ غَضَبٌ مِّن رَّبِّهِمْ وَذِلَّةٌ فِي الْحَيَاةِ الدُّنْيَا ۚ وَكَذَٰلِكَ نَجْزِي الْمُفْتَرِينَ
versets.tr: inna lladhīna ttakhadhū l-ʿijla sa-yanāluhum ghaḍabun min rabbihim wa-dhillatun fī l-ḥayāti d-dunyā wa-kadhālika najzī l-muftarīn
versets.trad: Truly, those who took the calf for worship have incurred the wrath of their Lord and humiliation in the life of this world. Thus do We requite those who fabricate lies.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: “wrath and humiliation” are the twofold penalty of idolatry: the wrath of the Lord forsaken and the abasement of one who lowered himself before a calf. Whoever forges a god with his hands sinks below the work of his own hands.

versets.ar: وَالَّذِينَ عَمِلُوا السَّيِّئَاتِ ثُمَّ تَابُوا مِن بَعْدِهَا وَآمَنُوا إِنَّ رَبَّكَ مِن بَعْدِهَا لَغَفُورٌ رَّحِيمٌ
versets.tr: wa-lladhīna ʿamilū s-sayyiʾāti thumma tābū min baʿdihā wa-āmanū inna rabbaka min baʿdihā la-ghafūrun raḥīm
versets.trad: As for those who committed evil deeds and then repented thereafter and believed, your Lord, after that, is surely Forgiving, Merciful.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: the door of repentance remains open after the worst fault; even the calf is not a sin beyond return. “Forgiving, Merciful” — these two Names lean over the penitent, effacing the past and enveloping in tenderness. Sincere return annuls all that preceded it.

versets.ar: وَلَمَّا سَكَتَ عَن مُّوسَى الْغَضَبُ أَخَذَ الْأَلْوَاحَ ۖ وَفِي نُسْخَتِهَا هُدًى وَرَحْمَةٌ لِّلَّذِينَ هُمْ لِرَبِّهِمْ يَرْهَبُونَ
versets.tr: wa-lammā sakata ʿan mūsā l-ghaḍabu akhadha l-alwāḥa wa-fī nuskhatihā hudan wa-raḥmatun li-lladhīna hum li-rabbihim yarhabūn
versets.trad: When the wrath of Moses had subsided, he took up the Tablets, in whose text there is guidance and mercy for those who hold their Lord in awe.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: “when the wrath fell silent” — the verb “to fall silent” says that wrath has a voice which must be hushed for wisdom to speak. Only when appeased does Mūsā take up the Tablets: guidance is not grasped in the tumult but in the recovered calm of the heart.

versets.ar: وَاخْتَارَ مُوسَىٰ قَوْمَهُ سَبْعِينَ رَجُلًا لِّمِيقَاتِنَا ۖ فَلَمَّا أَخَذَتْهُمُ الرَّجْفَةُ قَالَ رَبِّ لَوْ شِئْتَ أَهْلَكْتَهُم مِّن قَبْلُ وَإِيَّايَ ۖ أَتُهْلِكُنَا بِمَا فَعَلَ السُّفَهَاءُ مِنَّا ۖ إِنْ هِيَ إِلَّا فِتْنَتُكَ تُضِلُّ بِهَا مَن تَشَاءُ وَتَهْدِي مَن تَشَاءُ ۖ أَنتَ وَلِيُّنَا فَاغْفِرْ لَنَا وَارْحَمْنَا ۖ وَأَنتَ خَيْرُ الْغَافِرِينَ
versets.tr: wa-khtāra mūsā qawmahu sabʿīna rajulan li-mīqātinā fa-lammā akhadhathumu r-rajfatu qāla rabbi law shiʾta ahlaktahum min qablu wa-iyyāya a-tuhlikunā bimā faʿala s-sufahāʾu minnā in hiya illā fitnatuka tuḍillu bihā man tashāʾu wa-tahdī man tashāʾu anta waliyyunā fa-ghfir lanā wa-rḥamnā wa-anta khayru l-ghāfirīn
versets.trad: Moses chose from his people seventy men for Our meeting. When the earthquake seized them, he cried: “My Lord! Had You willed, You could have destroyed them before, and me with them. Will You destroy us for what the foolish among us have done? This is but Your trial, by which You let go astray whom You will and guide whom You will. You are our Protector! So forgive us and have mercy on us, for You are the best of those who forgive.
themes: the trial that guides or leads astray
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: “this is but Your trial: You let go astray whom You will and guide whom You will” — Mūsā surrenders all to the decree, acknowledging that guidance and straying are in the hands of God alone. This is the summit of submission: to seek the cause no longer outside the divine will, and to implore nothing but His forgiveness.

versets.ar: وَاكْتُبْ لَنَا فِي هَٰذِهِ الدُّنْيَا حَسَنَةً وَفِي الْآخِرَةِ إِنَّا هُدْنَا إِلَيْكَ ۚ قَالَ عَذَابِي أُصِيبُ بِهِ مَنْ أَشَاءُ ۖ وَرَحْمَتِي وَسِعَتْ كُلَّ شَيْءٍ ۚ فَسَأَكْتُبُهَا لِلَّذِينَ يَتَّقُونَ وَيُؤْتُونَ الزَّكَاةَ وَالَّذِينَ هُم بِآيَاتِنَا يُؤْمِنُونَ
versets.tr: wa-ktub lanā fī hādhihi d-dunyā ḥasanatan wa-fī l-ākhirati innā hudnā ilayka qāla ʿadhābī uṣību bihi man ashāʾu wa-raḥmatī wasiʿat kulla shayʾin fa-sa-aktubuhā li-lladhīna yattaqūna wa-yuʾtūna z-zakāta wa-lladhīna hum bi-āyātinā yuʾminūn
versets.trad: Inscribe for us a fair portion in this world and in the next. We have turned to You!” The Lord said: “My chastisement I inflict upon whom I will, and My mercy embraces all things. I shall inscribe it for those who are mindful of Me and give the alms, and those who believe in Our signs,
themes: the mercy that embraces all things
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: “My mercy embraces all things” (wasiʿat raḥmatī kulla shayʾ) — here is the vastest word of the Book: nothing escapes the raḥma, which precedes and overflows the chastisement. The chastisement is restricted — “whom I will” — but the mercy is without bounds; thus it is revealed that His gentleness infinitely outweighs His rigour.

mots.racine: ر ح م / و س ع

versets.ar: الَّذِينَ يَتَّبِعُونَ الرَّسُولَ النَّبِيَّ الْأُمِّيَّ الَّذِي يَجِدُونَهُ مَكْتُوبًا عِندَهُمْ فِي التَّوْرَاةِ وَالْإِنجِيلِ يَأْمُرُهُم بِالْمَعْرُوفِ وَيَنْهَاهُمْ عَنِ الْمُنكَرِ وَيُحِلُّ لَهُمُ الطَّيِّبَاتِ وَيُحَرِّمُ عَلَيْهِمُ الْخَبَائِثَ وَيَضَعُ عَنْهُمْ إِصْرَهُمْ وَالْأَغْلَالَ الَّتِي كَانَتْ عَلَيْهِمْ ۚ فَالَّذِينَ آمَنُوا بِهِ وَعَزَّرُوهُ وَنَصَرُوهُ وَاتَّبَعُوا النُّورَ الَّذِي أُنزِلَ مَعَهُ ۙ أُولَٰئِكَ هُمُ الْمُفْلِحُونَ
versets.tr: alladhīna yattabiʿūna r-rasūla n-nabiyya l-ummiyya lladhī yajidūnahu maktūban ʿindahum fī t-tawrāti wa-l-injīli yaʾmuruhum bi-l-maʿrūfi wa-yanhāhum ʿani l-munkari wa-yuḥillu lahumu ṭ-ṭayyibāti wa-yuḥarrimu ʿalayhimu l-khabāʾitha wa-yaḍaʿu ʿanhum iṣrahum wa-l-aghlāla llatī kānat ʿalayhim fa-lladhīna āmanū bihi wa-ʿazzarūhu wa-naṣarūhu wa-ttabaʿū n-nūra lladhī unzila maʿahu ulāʾika humu l-mufliḥūn
versets.trad: those who follow the Messenger, the unlettered Prophet, whom they find mentioned with them in the Torah and the Gospel. He enjoins upon them what is right and forbids them what is wrong; he makes lawful for them the good things and forbids them the impure; he relieves them of their burden and of the shackles that weighed upon them. Those who believe in him, who support him, who help him, and who follow the light sent down with him — those are the truly prosperous!”
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: “he relieves them of their burden and of the shackles” — the mission of the Prophet is liberating: he lightens, he unbinds, he opens. The light sent down with him adds no yoke but removes one. “Unlettered” (ummī), he is the unstained mirror upon which the Word is laid pure, unmixed with acquired learning.

mots.racine: ن ب أ / أ م م
mots.def: who can neither read nor write.

versets.ar: قُلْ يَا أَيُّهَا النَّاسُ إِنِّي رَسُولُ اللَّهِ إِلَيْكُمْ جَمِيعًا الَّذِي لَهُ مُلْكُ السَّمَاوَاتِ وَالْأَرْضِ ۖ لَا إِلَٰهَ إِلَّا هُوَ يُحْيِي وَيُمِيتُ ۖ فَآمِنُوا بِاللَّهِ وَرَسُولِهِ النَّبِيِّ الْأُمِّيِّ الَّذِي يُؤْمِنُ بِاللَّهِ وَكَلِمَاتِهِ وَاتَّبِعُوهُ لَعَلَّكُمْ تَهْتَدُونَ
versets.tr: qul yā ayyuhā n-nāsu innī rasūlu llāhi ilaykum jamīʿan alladhī lahu mulku s-samāwāti wa-l-arḍi lā ilāha illā huwa yuḥyī wa-yumītu fa-āminū bi-llāhi wa-rasūlihi n-nabiyyi l-ummiyyi lladhī yuʾminu bi-llāhi wa-kalimātihi wa-ttabiʿūhu laʿallakum tahtadūn
versets.trad: Say: “O mankind! I am indeed the Messenger of God to you all, of Him to whom belongs the kingdom of the heavens and the earth. There is no god but He. He gives life and gives death. So believe in God and in His Messenger, the unlettered Prophet, who believes in God and in His words; and follow him, that perhaps you may be guided.”
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: “to you all” — the mission of Muḥammad overflows every boundary of people or of time; he is the messenger to all humanity. And “he believes in God and in His words”: he is the first to believe in what he transmits, the exemplar before being the master. To follow him is to follow the one who follows God.

mots.racine: ح ي ي / م و ت

versets.ar: وَمِن قَوْمِ مُوسَىٰ أُمَّةٌ يَهْدُونَ بِالْحَقِّ وَبِهِ يَعْدِلُونَ
versets.tr: wa-min qawmi mūsā ummatun yahdūna bi-l-ḥaqqi wa-bihi yaʿdilūn
versets.trad: Among the people of Moses there is a community who guide by the Truth and who, by it, deal justly.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: even within a people that fails, God reserves “a community that guides by the Truth”; never is the earth empty of those who hold fast by the Truth. The small number of the faithful is the salt that preserves the mass from total corruption.

mots.racine: ه د ي / ح ق ق

versets.ar: وَقَطَّعْنَاهُمُ اثْنَتَيْ عَشْرَةَ أَسْبَاطًا أُمَمًا ۚ وَأَوْحَيْنَا إِلَىٰ مُوسَىٰ إِذِ اسْتَسْقَاهُ قَوْمُهُ أَنِ اضْرِب بِّعَصَاكَ الْحَجَرَ ۖ فَانبَجَسَتْ مِنْهُ اثْنَتَا عَشْرَةَ عَيْنًا ۖ قَدْ عَلِمَ كُلُّ أُنَاسٍ مَّشْرَبَهُمْ ۚ وَظَلَّلْنَا عَلَيْهِمُ الْغَمَامَ وَأَنزَلْنَا عَلَيْهِمُ الْمَنَّ وَالسَّلْوَىٰ ۖ كُلُوا مِن طَيِّبَاتِ مَا رَزَقْنَاكُمْ ۚ وَمَا ظَلَمُونَا وَلَٰكِن كَانُوا أَنفُسَهُمْ يَظْلِمُونَ
versets.tr: wa-qaṭṭaʿnāhumu thnatay ʿashrata asbāṭan umaman wa-awḥaynā ilā mūsā idhi stasqāhu qawmuhu ani-ḍrib bi-ʿaṣāka l-ḥajara fa-nbajasat minhu thnatā ʿashrata ʿaynan qad ʿalima kullu unāsin mashrabahum wa-ẓallalnā ʿalayhimu l-ghamāma wa-anzalnā ʿalayhimu l-manna wa-s-salwā kulū min ṭayyibāti mā razaqnākum wa-mā ẓalamūnā wa-lākin kānū anfusahum yaẓlimūn
versets.trad: We divided them into twelve tribes, twelve nations. And We revealed to Moses, when his people asked him for water: “Strike the rock with your staff!” Twelve springs gushed forth from it, and each of the groups knew its drinking place. We shaded them with the cloud and sent down upon them the manna and the quails: “Eat of the good things We have provided for you!” They did Us no wrong, but it was themselves they wronged.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: the water gushing from the rock, the shade of the cloud, the manna and the quails — a flood of graces upon ungrateful hearts. “It was not Us they wronged but themselves”: ingratitude does not reach the Giver, it wounds only the ingrate, depriving him of the fruit of the grace received.

mots.def: the descendants of the sons of Jacob.

mots.racine: م ن ن / س ل و

versets.ar: وَإِذْ قِيلَ لَهُمُ اسْكُنُوا هَٰذِهِ الْقَرْيَةَ وَكُلُوا مِنْهَا حَيْثُ شِئْتُمْ وَقُولُوا حِطَّةٌ وَادْخُلُوا الْبَابَ سُجَّدًا نَّغْفِرْ لَكُمْ خَطِيئَاتِكُمْ ۚ سَنَزِيدُ الْمُحْسِنِينَ
versets.tr: wa-idh qīla lahumu skunū hādhihi l-qaryata wa-kulū minhā ḥaythu shiʾtum wa-qūlū ḥiṭṭatun wa-dkhulū l-bāba sujjadan naghfir lakum khaṭīʾātikum sa-nazīdu l-muḥsinīn
versets.trad: They were told: “Dwell in this town and eat freely of whatever you find there. Say: “Relieve us!”, and enter its gate bowing low. Your faults shall be forgiven you, and We shall increase the reward of those who do good.”
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: to enter “bowing low” and to say “ḥiṭṭa” (lift our burden from us): the body's humility and the tongue's confession are the two keys of pardon. The gate of the town is an image of the gate of grace: one enters it only stooping, never with the head held high.

mots.def: “lift from us the load of sin.”

versets.ar: فَبَدَّلَ الَّذِينَ ظَلَمُوا مِنْهُمْ قَوْلًا غَيْرَ الَّذِي قِيلَ لَهُمْ فَأَرْسَلْنَا عَلَيْهِمْ رِجْزًا مِّنَ السَّمَاءِ بِمَا كَانُوا يَظْلِمُونَ
versets.tr: fa-baddala lladhīna ẓalamū minhum qawlan ghayra lladhī qīla lahum fa-arsalnā ʿalayhim rijzan mina s-samāʾi bimā kānū yaẓlimūn
versets.trad: But the wrongdoers among them substituted another word for the one they had been given, so We sent down upon them a chastisement from heaven as the price of their iniquity.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: they exchanged the word of pardon for a word of mockery, falsifying the confession prescribed; and it was by this alteration that they ruined themselves. To pervert the sacred word, even by a single term, is to betray the spirit of the command while pretending to keep its letter.

versets.ar: وَاسْأَلْهُمْ عَنِ الْقَرْيَةِ الَّتِي كَانَتْ حَاضِرَةَ الْبَحْرِ إِذْ يَعْدُونَ فِي السَّبْتِ إِذْ تَأْتِيهِمْ حِيتَانُهُمْ يَوْمَ سَبْتِهِمْ شُرَّعًا وَيَوْمَ لَا يَسْبِتُونَ ۙ لَا تَأْتِيهِمْ ۚ كَذَٰلِكَ نَبْلُوهُم بِمَا كَانُوا يَفْسُقُونَ
versets.tr: wa-sʾalhum ʿani l-qaryati llatī kānat ḥāḍirata l-baḥri idh yaʿdūna fī s-sabti idh taʾtīhim ḥītānuhum yawma sabtihim shurraʿan wa-yawma lā yasbitūna lā taʾtīhim kadhālika nablūhum bimā kānū yafsuqūn
versets.trad: Ask them about the town that stood by the sea, whose people transgressed the Sabbath. On that very day their great fish would rise to the surface of the water, while on days other than the Sabbath they did not show themselves. Thus did We try them, because they were perverse.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: the trial was subtle: the fish abounded precisely on the forbidden day. God often tries through what He forbids, placing the coveted good within the zone of the prohibited. The perversity lay not in desiring but in seeking stratagems to circumvent the command.

versets.ar: وَإِذْ قَالَتْ أُمَّةٌ مِّنْهُمْ لِمَ تَعِظُونَ قَوْمًا ۙ اللَّهُ مُهْلِكُهُمْ أَوْ مُعَذِّبُهُمْ عَذَابًا شَدِيدًا ۖ قَالُوا مَعْذِرَةً إِلَىٰ رَبِّكُمْ وَلَعَلَّهُمْ يَتَّقُونَ
versets.tr: wa-idh qālat ummatun minhum lima taʿiẓūna qawman allāhu muhlikuhum aw muʿadhdhibuhum ʿadhāban shadīdan qālū maʿdhiratan ilā rabbikum wa-laʿallahum yattaqūn
versets.trad: And when one community among them spoke up, saying: “Why do you admonish a people whom God is about to destroy or punish with a severe chastisement?”, the admonishers answered: “To be acquitted before your Lord, and so that perhaps they may come to fear God.”
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: “to be acquitted before your Lord” — to summon to the good remains a duty even when hope seems faint; one does not fall silent because another appears lost. The reminder absolves the one who gives it and keeps open the door of repentance for the one who receives it.

versets.ar: فَلَمَّا نَسُوا مَا ذُكِّرُوا بِهِ أَنجَيْنَا الَّذِينَ يَنْهَوْنَ عَنِ السُّوءِ وَأَخَذْنَا الَّذِينَ ظَلَمُوا بِعَذَابٍ بَئِيسٍ بِمَا كَانُوا يَفْسُقُونَ
versets.tr: fa-lammā nasū mā dhukkirū bihi anjaynā lladhīna yanhawna ʿani s-sūʾi wa-akhadhnā lladhīna ẓalamū bi-ʿadhābin baʾīsin bimā kānū yafsuqūn
versets.trad: But when they had forgotten what they had been reminded of, We saved those who forbade evil and seized the wrongdoers with a grievous chastisement, as the price of their perversity.
themes: the deliverance of those who forbid evil
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: saved were “those who forbade evil”: the bidding of good is not in vain, though it bear no visible fruit; it first saves the one who carries it. The word of admonition, even when rejected, raises a barrier between the reminder and the chastisement.

mots.racine: ن ه ي / س و أ

versets.ar: فَلَمَّا عَتَوْا عَن مَّا نُهُوا عَنْهُ قُلْنَا لَهُمْ كُونُوا قِرَدَةً خَاسِئِينَ
versets.tr: fa-lammā ʿataw ʿan mā nuhū ʿanhu qulnā lahum kūnū qiradatan khāsiʾīn
versets.trad: And when they rebelled against what had been forbidden them, We said to them: “Be despised apes!”
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: their transformation into apes is the outward manifestation of an abasement already inward: he who debases himself through transgression discloses without the very form of his soul. The chastisement only makes visible the bestiality that sin had already inscribed within the heart.

versets.ar: وَإِذْ تَأَذَّنَ رَبُّكَ لَيَبْعَثَنَّ عَلَيْهِمْ إِلَىٰ يَوْمِ الْقِيَامَةِ مَن يَسُومُهُمْ سُوءَ الْعَذَابِ ۗ إِنَّ رَبَّكَ لَسَرِيعُ الْعِقَابِ ۖ وَإِنَّهُ لَغَفُورٌ رَّحِيمٌ
versets.tr: wa-idh taʾadhdhana rabbuka la-yabʿathanna ʿalayhim ilā yawmi l-qiyāmati man yasūmuhum sūʾa l-ʿadhābi inna rabbaka la-sarīʿu l-ʿiqābi wa-innahu la-ghafūrun raḥīm
versets.trad: Then your Lord proclaimed that He would send against them, until the Day of Resurrection, those who would inflict upon them a grievous chastisement. Your Lord is swift to punish, and He is, in truth, forgiving, merciful.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: “swift to punish... forgiving, merciful” — the two faces stand within the same verse: severity for the one who persists, gentleness for the one who returns. God never raises the threat without at once reopening the door of mercy; forgiveness always has the last word.

mots.racine: س ر ع / ع ق ب
غ ف ر / ر ح م
mots.def: who covers over and shows mercy.

versets.ar: وَقَطَّعْنَاهُمْ فِي الْأَرْضِ أُمَمًا ۖ مِّنْهُمُ الصَّالِحُونَ وَمِنْهُمْ دُونَ ذَٰلِكَ ۖ وَبَلَوْنَاهُم بِالْحَسَنَاتِ وَالسَّيِّئَاتِ لَعَلَّهُمْ يَرْجِعُونَ
versets.tr: wa-qaṭṭaʿnāhum fī l-arḍi umaman minhumu ṣ-ṣāliḥūna wa-minhum dūna dhālika wa-balawnāhum bi-l-ḥasanāti wa-s-sayyiʾāti laʿallahum yarjiʿūn
versets.trad: We scattered them across the earth in communities: some of them are righteous, and others are not. We tried them with good things and with evil ones, that perhaps they might return to Us.
themes: the trial through good and evil
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: “We tried them with goods and with ills” — the trial of ease is subtler than the trial of hardship; many whom adversity brings back, prosperity leads astray. Both have one same end: “that they might return.” All in life is a summons to the return.

versets.ar: فَخَلَفَ مِن بَعْدِهِمْ خَلْفٌ وَرِثُوا الْكِتَابَ يَأْخُذُونَ عَرَضَ هَٰذَا الْأَدْنَىٰ وَيَقُولُونَ سَيُغْفَرُ لَنَا وَإِن يَأْتِهِمْ عَرَضٌ مِّثْلُهُ يَأْخُذُوهُ ۚ أَلَمْ يُؤْخَذْ عَلَيْهِم مِّيثَاقُ الْكِتَابِ أَن لَّا يَقُولُوا عَلَى اللَّهِ إِلَّا الْحَقَّ وَدَرَسُوا مَا فِيهِ ۗ وَالدَّارُ الْآخِرَةُ خَيْرٌ لِّلَّذِينَ يَتَّقُونَ ۗ أَفَلَا تَعْقِلُونَ
versets.tr: fa-khalafa min baʿdihim khalfun warithū l-kitāba yaʾkhudhūna ʿaraḍa hādhā l-adnā wa-yaqūlūna sa-yughfaru lanā wa-in yaʾtihim ʿaraḍun mithluhu yaʾkhudhūhu a-lam yuʾkhadh ʿalayhim mīthāqu l-kitābi an lā yaqūlū ʿalā llāhi illā l-ḥaqqa wa-darasū mā fīhi wa-d-dāru l-ākhiratu khayrun li-lladhīna yattaqūna a-fa-lā taʿqilūn
versets.trad: After them came successors who inherited the Book. They seized the goods of this lower world, saying: “It will be forgiven us!” And whenever the like of those goods came their way, they seized them again. Had they not contracted the covenant of the Book, that they should say of God nothing but the truth, having studied what is in it? The abode of the Hereafter is better for those who fear God — will you not understand?
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: “it will be forgiven us” becomes the watchword of the one who sins with deliberate intent; they turn the hope of pardon into a license to transgress. To presume upon mercy in order to permit oneself sin is the corruption of hope itself, which ought to restrain, not loosen.

mots.racine: و ث ق / ك ت ب

versets.ar: وَالَّذِينَ يُمَسِّكُونَ بِالْكِتَابِ وَأَقَامُوا الصَّلَاةَ إِنَّا لَا نُضِيعُ أَجْرَ الْمُصْلِحِينَ
versets.tr: wa-lladhīna yumassikūna bi-l-kitābi wa-aqāmū ṣ-ṣalāta innā lā nuḍīʿu ajra l-muṣliḥīn
versets.trad: As for those who hold fast to the Book and keep up the prayer — truly, We do not let the reward of those who set things right be lost.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: “to hold fast to the Book” — not with the fingertips but with both hands, and to join to it the established prayer. God then guarantees: “We do not let the wage of the reformers be lost.” Nothing done for Him is lost; the least act of faithfulness is recorded and preserved.

mots.racine: ق و م / ص ل و

mots.sens: those who set things right, reformers

versets.ar: وَإِذْ نَتَقْنَا الْجَبَلَ فَوْقَهُمْ كَأَنَّهُ ظُلَّةٌ وَظَنُّوا أَنَّهُ وَاقِعٌ بِهِمْ خُذُوا مَا آتَيْنَاكُم بِقُوَّةٍ وَاذْكُرُوا مَا فِيهِ لَعَلَّكُمْ تَتَّقُونَ
versets.tr: wa-idh nataqnā l-jabala fawqahum ka-annahu ẓullatun wa-ẓannū annahu wāqiʿun bihim khudhū mā ātaynākum bi-quwwatin wa-dhkurū mā fīhi laʿallakum tattaqūn
versets.trad: Remember when We raised the Mount above them like a canopy, and they thought it was about to fall upon them. [We said:] “Hold fast to what We have given you, and remember what it contains; perhaps you will fear God.”
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: the mountain suspended above them is the image of the divine majesty that overhangs the covenant; the pact is contracted under the weight of reverential awe. To receive the Law “with strength” beneath that threatening shadow engraves in the heart that the Real is not taken lightly.

versets.ar: وَإِذْ أَخَذَ رَبُّكَ مِن بَنِي آدَمَ مِن ظُهُورِهِمْ ذُرِّيَّتَهُمْ وَأَشْهَدَهُمْ عَلَىٰ أَنفُسِهِمْ أَلَسْتُ بِرَبِّكُمْ ۖ قَالُوا بَلَىٰ ۛ شَهِدْنَا ۛ أَن تَقُولُوا يَوْمَ الْقِيَامَةِ إِنَّا كُنَّا عَنْ هَٰذَا غَافِلِينَ
versets.tr: wa-idh akhadha rabbuka min banī ādama min ẓuhūrihim dhurriyyatahum wa-ashhadahum ʿalā anfusihim a-lastu bi-rabbikum qālū balā shahidnā an taqūlū yawma l-qiyāmati innā kunnā ʿan hādhā ghāfilīn
versets.trad: When your Lord drew forth from the loins of the children of Adam all their descendants, and made them bear witness against themselves: “Am I not your Lord?” — they said: “Yes indeed! We bear witness.” [This was] lest you should say on the Day of Resurrection: “We were unaware of this.”
versets.commentaire: Secret heart of the sura. Qushayrī: “Am I not your Lord? — Yes, we bear witness” (alastu bi-rabbikum / balā): this is the Covenant from before time, where all souls, drawn forth from the loins of Ādam, acknowledged the lordship of God before they were even born. That primordial “yes” lies buried in every heart like an ember beneath the ash; faith is nothing but the awakening of this first memory, and the longing for God is nothing but the echo of that day when He spoke to us and we knew Him.

mots.def: the address of the primordial Covenant.

versets.ar: أَوْ تَقُولُوا إِنَّمَا أَشْرَكَ آبَاؤُنَا مِن قَبْلُ وَكُنَّا ذُرِّيَّةً مِّن بَعْدِهِمْ ۖ أَفَتُهْلِكُنَا بِمَا فَعَلَ الْمُبْطِلُونَ
versets.tr: aw taqūlū innamā ashraka ābāʾunā min qablu wa-kunnā dhurriyyatan min baʿdihim a-fa-tuhlikunā bimā faʿala l-mubṭilūn
versets.trad: Or lest you should say: “It was our fathers before us who associated others with God, and we were only their descendants who came after them. Would You then destroy us for what the followers of falsehood did?”
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: none shall be able to excuse themselves by the fault of their fathers, for the Covenant was sealed in each soul directly; everyone heard and answered “yes” on their own account. The inheritance of error does not dispense one from faithfulness to the inward oath that every heart bears.

versets.ar: وَكَذَٰلِكَ نُفَصِّلُ الْآيَاتِ وَلَعَلَّهُمْ يَرْجِعُونَ
versets.tr: wa-kadhālika nufaṣṣilu l-āyāti wa-laʿallahum yarjiʿūn
versets.trad: Thus do We expound the signs in detail, that perhaps they may return to Us.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: all the detailing of the signs aims at one thing alone: “that they may return.” The reminder of the Covenant is itself a call to return toward that first “yes”; each verse is a hand held out to lead the soul back to the memory of its acknowledged lordship.

versets.ar: وَاتْلُ عَلَيْهِمْ نَبَأَ الَّذِي آتَيْنَاهُ آيَاتِنَا فَانسَلَخَ مِنْهَا فَأَتْبَعَهُ الشَّيْطَانُ فَكَانَ مِنَ الْغَاوِينَ
versets.tr: wa-tlu ʿalayhim nabaʾa lladhī ātaynāhu āyātinā fa-nsalakha minhā fa-atbaʿahu sh-shayṭānu fa-kāna mina l-ghāwīn
versets.trad: Recite to them the tale of the one to whom We gave Our signs, yet he stripped himself away from them; so Satan pursued him, and he became one of the lost.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: “he stripped himself away from Our signs” (insalakha) as the serpent sheds its skin: a terrible image of one who possessed knowledge and cast it off. Learning is no rampart if the heart cleaves to the lower world; knowledge abandoned by the soul becomes the greatest of all errings.

mots.def: shed them as one sheds a skin.

versets.ar: وَلَوْ شِئْنَا لَرَفَعْنَاهُ بِهَا وَلَٰكِنَّهُ أَخْلَدَ إِلَى الْأَرْضِ وَاتَّبَعَ هَوَاهُ ۚ فَمَثَلُهُ كَمَثَلِ الْكَلْبِ إِن تَحْمِلْ عَلَيْهِ يَلْهَثْ أَوْ تَتْرُكْهُ يَلْهَث ۚ ذَّٰلِكَ مَثَلُ الْقَوْمِ الَّذِينَ كَذَّبُوا بِآيَاتِنَا ۚ فَاقْصُصِ الْقَصَصَ لَعَلَّهُمْ يَتَفَكَّرُونَ
versets.tr: wa-law shiʾnā la-rafaʿnāhu bihā wa-lākinnahu akhlada ilā l-arḍi wa-ttabaʿa hawāhu fa-mathaluhu ka-mathali l-kalbi in taḥmil ʿalayhi yalhath aw tatrukhu yalhath dhālika mathalu l-qawmi lladhīna kadhdhabū bi-āyātinā fa-qṣuṣi l-qaṣaṣa laʿallahum yatafakkarūn
versets.trad: Had We willed, We would have raised him up through those signs; but he clung to the earth and followed his cravings. He was like a dog: whether you drive it off, it lolls out its tongue panting, or whether you leave it be, it pants still. Such is the likeness of the people who deny Our signs. So recount the tales, that perhaps they may reflect.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: “he clung to the earth and followed his craving” — here is the spring of every fall: to prefer the low to the high, the perishable to the eternal. And the image of the dog that pants ceaselessly, whether driven off or left alone, paints the greedy soul that is never sated: covetousness is a thirst that nothing quenches, a perpetual panting.

mots.racine: خ ل د / أ ر ض

mots.def: lolls out its tongue from thirst.

versets.ar: سَاءَ مَثَلًا الْقَوْمُ الَّذِينَ كَذَّبُوا بِآيَاتِنَا وَأَنفُسَهُمْ كَانُوا يَظْلِمُونَ
versets.tr: sāʾa mathalan al-qawmu lladhīna kadhdhabū bi-āyātinā wa-anfusahum kānū yaẓlimūn
versets.trad: Evil is the likeness of the people who deny Our signs and who wrong their own souls.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: “it was themselves they wronged” — denial does not wound the signs, which remain true, but the denier, who deprives himself of their light. To give the lie is a spiritual suicide: one does not extinguish the sun by shutting the eyes, one only condemns oneself to the dark.

mots.racine: س و أ / م ث ل

versets.ar: مَن يَهْدِ اللَّهُ فَهُوَ الْمُهْتَدِي ۖ وَمَن يُضْلِلْ فَأُولَٰئِكَ هُمُ الْخَاسِرُونَ
versets.tr: man yahdi llāhu fa-huwa l-muhtadī wa-man yuḍlil fa-ulāʾika humu l-khāsirūn
versets.trad: Whomever God guides is rightly guided; and those whom He leaves to stray, they are the losers.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: guidance and straying belong to God alone; none guides himself by his own strength, and none is lost save by a deserved divine forsaking. The servant has only to stretch out his hand toward the light and to acknowledge that it is He who holds it out to him.

versets.ar: وَلَقَدْ ذَرَأْنَا لِجَهَنَّمَ كَثِيرًا مِّنَ الْجِنِّ وَالْإِنسِ ۖ لَهُمْ قُلُوبٌ لَّا يَفْقَهُونَ بِهَا وَلَهُمْ أَعْيُنٌ لَّا يُبْصِرُونَ بِهَا وَلَهُمْ آذَانٌ لَّا يَسْمَعُونَ بِهَا ۚ أُولَٰئِكَ كَالْأَنْعَامِ بَلْ هُمْ أَضَلُّ ۚ أُولَٰئِكَ هُمُ الْغَافِلُونَ
versets.tr: wa-la-qad dharaʾnā li-jahannama kathīran mina l-jinni wa-l-insi lahum qulūbun lā yafqahūna bihā wa-lahum aʿyunun lā yubṣirūna bihā wa-lahum ādhānun lā yasmaʿūna bihā ulāʾika ka-l-anʿāmi bal hum aḍallu ulāʾika humu l-ghāfilūn
versets.trad: We have destined for Hell a great many of the jinn and of mankind. They have hearts with which they do not understand, eyes with which they do not see, ears with which they do not hear. Such are like cattle — nay, more astray still. Such are the heedless.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: they have heart, eyes, ears, yet inert: the organs of spiritual perception, which they do not employ. “Worse than cattle,” for the beast follows its nature without deviation, while man, endowed with reason, betrays his. Heedlessness (ghafla) is to possess the instruments of knowledge and to live as though they did not exist.

versets.ar: وَلِلَّهِ الْأَسْمَاءُ الْحُسْنَىٰ فَادْعُوهُ بِهَا ۖ وَذَرُوا الَّذِينَ يُلْحِدُونَ فِي أَسْمَائِهِ ۚ سَيُجْزَوْنَ مَا كَانُوا يَعْمَلُونَ
versets.tr: wa-li-llāhi l-asmāʾu l-ḥusnā fa-dʿūhu bihā wa-dharū lladhīna yulḥidūna fī asmāʾihi sa-yujzawna mā kānū yaʿmalūn
versets.trad: To God belong the most beautiful Names. Call upon Him by them. And turn away from those who profane His Names; they shall be requited for what they used to do.
versets.commentaire: Verse of the Beautiful Names (al-asmāʾ al-ḥusnā). Qushayrī: “call upon Him by them” — each Name is a door of access and a secret of nearness; whoever invokes by the Name that answers to his state finds the response that suits him. The sick call upon the Healer, the lost upon the Guide, the sinner upon the Forgiver. The Names are the rays by which the inaccessible Essence allows itself to be approached and named.

mots.racine: س م و / ح س ن

versets.ar: وَمِمَّنْ خَلَقْنَا أُمَّةٌ يَهْدُونَ بِالْحَقِّ وَبِهِ يَعْدِلُونَ
versets.tr: wa-mimman khalaqnā ummatun yahdūna bi-l-ḥaqqi wa-bihi yaʿdilūn
versets.trad: Among those We have created there is a community who guide by the Truth and by it dispense justice.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: in every age there remains “a community who guide by the Real”; these are the heirs of the light, those who uphold justice upon the foundation of Truth. Their existence is a grace for the world: through them the earth keeps a heart faithful to the Covenant.

mots.racine: ه د ي / ح ق ق

versets.ar: وَالَّذِينَ كَذَّبُوا بِآيَاتِنَا سَنَسْتَدْرِجُهُم مِّنْ حَيْثُ لَا يَعْلَمُونَ
versets.tr: wa-lladhīna kadhdhabū bi-āyātinā sa-nastadrijuhum min ḥaythu lā yaʿlamūn
versets.trad: As for those who deny Our signs, We shall lead them on by degrees, from whence they do not know.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: istidrāj is the subtlest of chastisements: God heaps upon the denier favors that sink him little by little, degree after degree, without his suspecting it. Each grace ill-received draws him nearer the abyss; he believes he prospers while he goes astray. Dread the favor that does not bring you nearer to Him.

mots.sens: We shall lead them on by degrees
from whence they do not know
mots.def: from where they do not suspect.

versets.ar: وَأُمْلِي لَهُمْ ۚ إِنَّ كَيْدِي مَتِينٌ
versets.tr: wa-umlī lahum inna kaydī matīn
versets.trad: And I grant them respite. Truly, My stratagem is firm.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: “I grant them respite” — the reprieve is not forgetfulness but part of the design; “My stratagem is firm” means that the delay itself works toward the fulfillment of the decree. Let no one rejoice in a long reprieve: it may be only the rope let out.

versets.ar: أَوَلَمْ يَتَفَكَّرُوا ۗ مَا بِصَاحِبِهِم مِّن جِنَّةٍ ۚ إِنْ هُوَ إِلَّا نَذِيرٌ مُّبِينٌ
versets.tr: a-wa-lam yatafakkarū mā bi-ṣāḥibihim min jinnatin in huwa illā nadhīrun mubīn
versets.trad: Have they not reflected? Their companion is not possessed; he is only a clear warner.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: “their companion” — God reminds them that they know him, that he has lived among them; the charge of madness falls of itself for the one who reflects. Reflection (tafakkur) dispels the calumny: the heart that meditates recognizes the truth of the one it has always known faithful.

mots.racine: ن ذ ر / ب ي ن

versets.ar: أَوَلَمْ يَنظُرُوا فِي مَلَكُوتِ السَّمَاوَاتِ وَالْأَرْضِ وَمَا خَلَقَ اللَّهُ مِن شَيْءٍ وَأَنْ عَسَىٰ أَن يَكُونَ قَدِ اقْتَرَبَ أَجَلُهُمْ ۖ فَبِأَيِّ حَدِيثٍ بَعْدَهُ يُؤْمِنُونَ
versets.tr: a-wa-lam yanẓurū fī malakūti s-samāwāti wa-l-arḍi wa-mā khalaqa llāhu min shayʾin wa-an ʿasā an yakūna qadi qtaraba ajaluhum fa-bi-ayyi ḥadīthin baʿdahu yuʾminūn
versets.trad: Have they not gazed into the Dominion of the heavens and the earth and all that God has created, and considered that their term may well be drawing near? In what discourse, after this, will they believe?
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: “to gaze into the malakūt” — the malakūt is the hidden face of things, their dimension as a divine kingdom; to see beyond the appearance is to read in every creature the sign of its Creator. Whoever contemplates thus, and ponders the nearness of his term, can doubt no longer; after this, what discourse could still persuade him?

mots.def: the invisible kingdom, the divine face of things.

versets.ar: مَن يُضْلِلِ اللَّهُ فَلَا هَادِيَ لَهُ ۚ وَيَذَرُهُمْ فِي طُغْيَانِهِمْ يَعْمَهُونَ
versets.tr: man yuḍlili llāhu fa-lā hādiya lahu wa-yadharuhum fī ṭughyānihim yaʿmahūn
versets.trad: Whomever God leaves to stray has no guide. He abandons them, wandering blindly in their rebellion.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: “wandering blindly in their rebellion” — the deepest straying is to grope without even seeking the guide, content with one's wandering. He whom God leaves to himself turns in circles in his excess, mistaking his blindness for freedom.

versets.ar: يَسْأَلُونَكَ عَنِ السَّاعَةِ أَيَّانَ مُرْسَاهَا ۖ قُلْ إِنَّمَا عِلْمُهَا عِندَ رَبِّي ۖ لَا يُجَلِّيهَا لِوَقْتِهَا إِلَّا هُوَ ۚ ثَقُلَتْ فِي السَّمَاوَاتِ وَالْأَرْضِ ۚ لَا تَأْتِيكُمْ إِلَّا بَغْتَةً ۗ يَسْأَلُونَكَ كَأَنَّكَ حَفِيٌّ عَنْهَا ۖ قُلْ إِنَّمَا عِلْمُهَا عِندَ اللَّهِ وَلَٰكِنَّ أَكْثَرَ النَّاسِ لَا يَعْلَمُونَ
versets.tr: yasʾalūnaka ʿani s-sāʿati ayyāna mursāhā qul innamā ʿilmuhā ʿinda rabbī lā yujallīhā li-waqtihā illā huwa thaqulat fī s-samāwāti wa-l-arḍi lā taʾtīkum illā baghtatan yasʾalūnaka ka-annaka ḥafiyyun ʿanhā qul innamā ʿilmuhā ʿinda llāhi wa-lākinna akthara n-nāsi lā yaʿlamūn
versets.trad: They ask you about the Hour, when it will come. Say: “Its knowledge belongs only to my Lord; none but He will disclose it at its appointed time. It weighs heavy upon the heavens and the earth; it will not come upon you save suddenly.” They ask you as though you were intimately informed of it. Say: “Its knowledge belongs only to God, but most people do not know.”
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: the knowledge of the Hour is a secret God reserves to Himself; even the Prophet does not know it. This not-knowing is no lack but wisdom: the uncertainty of the term keeps the heart awake. Were the hour known, one would defer the return; to veil it is a mercy that presses toward vigilance.

versets.ar: قُل لَّا أَمْلِكُ لِنَفْسِي نَفْعًا وَلَا ضَرًّا إِلَّا مَا شَاءَ اللَّهُ ۚ وَلَوْ كُنتُ أَعْلَمُ الْغَيْبَ لَاسْتَكْثَرْتُ مِنَ الْخَيْرِ وَمَا مَسَّنِيَ السُّوءُ ۚ إِنْ أَنَا إِلَّا نَذِيرٌ وَبَشِيرٌ لِّقَوْمٍ يُؤْمِنُونَ
versets.tr: qul lā amliku li-nafsī nafʿan wa-lā ḍarran illā mā shāʾa llāhu wa-law kuntu aʿlamu l-ghayba la-stakthartu mina l-khayri wa-mā massaniya s-sūʾu in anā illā nadhīrun wa-bashīrun li-qawmin yuʾminūn
versets.trad: Say: “Of myself I have no power to bring benefit or harm, except as God wills. Had I known the Unseen, I would have amassed an abundance of good, and no evil would have touched me. But I am only a warner and a bearer of glad tidings to a people who believe.”
themes: warner and bearer of glad tidings
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: even the Prophet proclaims his powerlessness and his ignorance of the Unseen (ghayb); it is the very summit of servanthood (ʿubūdiyya) to refer all to the will of God. The higher one rises in nearness, the more one confesses one's dependence; the greatness of the servant lies in the avowal of his nothingness before the Lord.

versets.ar: هُوَ الَّذِي خَلَقَكُم مِّن نَّفْسٍ وَاحِدَةٍ وَجَعَلَ مِنْهَا زَوْجَهَا لِيَسْكُنَ إِلَيْهَا ۖ فَلَمَّا تَغَشَّاهَا حَمَلَتْ حَمْلًا خَفِيفًا فَمَرَّتْ بِهِ ۖ فَلَمَّا أَثْقَلَت دَّعَوَا اللَّهَ رَبَّهُمَا لَئِنْ آتَيْتَنَا صَالِحًا لَّنَكُونَنَّ مِنَ الشَّاكِرِينَ
versets.tr: huwa lladhī khalaqakum min nafsin wāḥidatin wa-jaʿala minhā zawjahā li-yaskuna ilayhā fa-lammā taghashshāhā ḥamalat ḥamlan khafīfan fa-marrat bihi fa-lammā athqalat daʿawā llāha rabbahumā la-ʾin ātaytanā ṣāliḥan la-nakūnanna mina sh-shākirīn
versets.trad: It is He who created you from a single soul, and from it brought forth its mate, that he might find repose with her. When he has covered her, she bears a light burden and goes about with it. Then, when she grows heavy, the two call upon God, their Lord: “If You grant us a well-formed child, we shall surely be among the thankful.”
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: “that he might find repose with her” (li-yaskuna ilayhā) — the repose (sukūn) of the couple is a sign of the divine mercy inscribed within creation; the soul seeks in the other a stillness that, at bottom, is only the echo of the supreme repose in God. And in distress the couple turns instinctively toward Him: the trial reveals the dependence.

mots.racine: ن ف س / و ح د

mots.sens: that he might find repose with her

versets.ar: فَلَمَّا آتَاهُمَا صَالِحًا جَعَلَا لَهُ شُرَكَاءَ فِيمَا آتَاهُمَا ۚ فَتَعَالَى اللَّهُ عَمَّا يُشْرِكُونَ
versets.tr: fa-lammā ātāhumā ṣāliḥan jaʿalā lahu shurakāʾa fīmā ātāhumā fa-taʿālā llāhu ʿammā yushrikūn
versets.trad: Yet when He has granted them a well-formed child, the two assign Him associates in what He has bestowed upon them. Exalted be God above what they associate with Him!
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: in distress they invoked none but Him; scarcely answered, they assign Him associates. Such is the fickleness of the soul: sincere in trial, idolatrous in ease. The sincerity of the cry under necessity is worth nothing unless it persists in the favor received.

versets.ar: أَيُشْرِكُونَ مَا لَا يَخْلُقُ شَيْئًا وَهُمْ يُخْلَقُونَ
versets.tr: a-yushrikūna mā lā yakhluqu shayʾan wa-hum yukhlaqūn
versets.trad: Will they associate with Him deities that create nothing, while they themselves are created,
themes: the absurdity of the created idol
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: what mockery to worship what is created like oneself! The idol creates nothing; it is itself fashioned by the hand that adores it. To associate with the Creator a created thing is to confound the workman with his work, the potter with his pot.

versets.ar: وَلَا يَسْتَطِيعُونَ لَهُمْ نَصْرًا وَلَا أَنفُسَهُمْ يَنصُرُونَ
versets.tr: wa-lā yastaṭīʿūna lahum naṣran wa-lā anfusahum yanṣurūn
versets.trad: and that can neither help them nor help themselves?
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: they can succor neither another nor themselves: what can the one who prays to the idol hope from it? To implore the aid of what does not aid itself is the height of blindness. Worthy of invocation is He alone who holds all power.

versets.ar: وَإِن تَدْعُوهُمْ إِلَى الْهُدَىٰ لَا يَتَّبِعُوكُمْ ۚ سَوَاءٌ عَلَيْكُمْ أَدَعَوْتُمُوهُمْ أَمْ أَنتُمْ صَامِتُونَ
versets.tr: wa-in tadʿūhum ilā l-hudā lā yattabiʿūkum sawāʾun ʿalaykum a-daʿawtumūhum am antum ṣāmitūn
versets.trad: If you call them to right guidance, they will not follow you. It is all one to them whether you call upon them or remain silent.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: “it is all one to them whether you call upon them or keep silent” — the idol answers neither the cry nor the silence; it is pure inertia. What a contrast with God, who answers before He is even prayed to, and whom the silence of the heart reaches as much as the spoken word.

versets.ar: إِنَّ الَّذِينَ تَدْعُونَ مِن دُونِ اللَّهِ عِبَادٌ أَمْثَالُكُمْ ۖ فَادْعُوهُمْ فَلْيَسْتَجِيبُوا لَكُمْ إِن كُنتُمْ صَادِقِينَ
versets.tr: inna lladhīna tadʿūna min dūni llāhi ʿibādun amthālukum fa-dʿūhum fa-l-yastajībū lakum in kuntum ṣādiqīn
versets.trad: Those whom you call upon apart from God are but servants like yourselves. Call upon them, then, and let them answer you, if you are truthful!
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: “servants like yourselves” — all that is worshipped apart from God is, like the worshipper, created and dependent. The challenge is cutting: let them answer you, then, if you believe in their divinity! But the silence of the idol confounds the lie of the one who prays to it.

mots.racine: ع ب د / م ث ل

versets.ar: أَلَهُمْ أَرْجُلٌ يَمْشُونَ بِهَا ۖ أَمْ لَهُمْ أَيْدٍ يَبْطِشُونَ بِهَا ۖ أَمْ لَهُمْ أَعْيُنٌ يُبْصِرُونَ بِهَا ۖ أَمْ لَهُمْ آذَانٌ يَسْمَعُونَ بِهَا ۗ قُلِ ادْعُوا شُرَكَاءَكُمْ ثُمَّ كِيدُونِ فَلَا تُنظِرُونِ
versets.tr: a-lahum arjulun yamshūna bihā am lahum aydin yabṭishūna bihā am lahum aʿyunun yubṣirūna bihā am lahum ādhānun yasmaʿūna bihā quli-dʿū shurakāʾakum thumma kīdūni fa-lā tunẓirūn
versets.trad: Have they feet with which to walk? Or hands with which to grasp? Or eyes with which to see? Or ears with which to hear? Say: “Call upon your associates, then scheme against me and grant me no respite!”
themes: the utter powerlessness of the idols
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: the enumeration of the idol's inert limbs — feet, hands, eyes, ears all motionless — underscores by contrast the living attributes of God. And the challenge “scheme against me without respite” shows the assurance of one who leans upon the Living: no plot can reach him, for his Protector neither sleeps nor falters.

versets.ar: إِنَّ وَلِيِّيَ اللَّهُ الَّذِي نَزَّلَ الْكِتَابَ ۖ وَهُوَ يَتَوَلَّى الصَّالِحِينَ
versets.tr: inna waliyyiya llāhu lladhī nazzala l-kitāba wa-huwa yatawallā ṣ-ṣāliḥīn
versets.trad: Truly, my Protector is God, who has sent down the Book; and it is He who takes the righteous into His care.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: “my Protector (waliyy) is God” — here is the rock on which the believer stands in the face of every threat: no other ally, no other support. “He takes the righteous into His care”: whoever entrusts himself to Him is never given over to the enemy, for the divine walāya enfolds and defends.

mots.racine: ن ز ل / ك ت ب

versets.ar: وَالَّذِينَ تَدْعُونَ مِن دُونِهِ لَا يَسْتَطِيعُونَ نَصْرَكُمْ وَلَا أَنفُسَهُمْ يَنصُرُونَ
versets.tr: wa-lladhīna tadʿūna min dūnihi lā yastaṭīʿūna naṣrakum wa-lā anfusahum yanṣurūn
versets.trad: Those whom you call upon apart from Him can neither help you nor help themselves.
themes: the nullity of the false gods
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: repeated to fix the lesson: what cannot succor itself cannot succor you. The opposition is total between the real Protector and the illusory protectors; between Him who takes into His care and those who can do nothing, even for themselves.

versets.ar: وَإِن تَدْعُوهُمْ إِلَى الْهُدَىٰ لَا يَسْمَعُوا ۖ وَتَرَاهُمْ يَنظُرُونَ إِلَيْكَ وَهُمْ لَا يُبْصِرُونَ
versets.tr: wa-in tadʿūhum ilā l-hudā lā yasmaʿū wa-tarāhum yanẓurūna ilayka wa-hum lā yubṣirūn
versets.trad: If you call them to right guidance, they do not hear. You see them looking toward you, yet they do not see.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: “they look toward you without seeing” — there is a looking that is not seeing, eyes wide open upon blindness. So with the idols and their painted eyes, so with veiled hearts: the outward sight remains when the inner vision (baṣīra) has gone out.

versets.ar: خُذِ الْعَفْوَ وَأْمُرْ بِالْعُرْفِ وَأَعْرِضْ عَنِ الْجَاهِلِينَ
versets.tr: khudhi l-ʿafwa wa-ʾmur bi-l-ʿurfi wa-aʿriḍ ʿani l-jāhilīn
versets.trad: Hold to forbearance, enjoin what is good, and turn away from the ignorant.
themes: the threefold wisdom: pardon, good, forbearance
versets.commentaire: Verse the Sufis call “the verse of noble character.” Qushayrī: “take what is freely given” (khudhi l-ʿafwa) — accept from people what they offer with ease, without forcing them; enjoin what is fitting with gentleness; turn away from the ignorant without combating him. These three maxims sum up the whole ethic of the accomplished man: forbearance, exhortation, peaceful withdrawal.

mots.def: that which is recognized as good.

versets.ar: وَإِمَّا يَنزَغَنَّكَ مِنَ الشَّيْطَانِ نَزْغٌ فَاسْتَعِذْ بِاللَّهِ ۚ إِنَّهُ سَمِيعٌ عَلِيمٌ
