versets.tr: wa-immā yanzaghannaka mina sh-shayṭāni nazghun fa-staʿidh bi-llāhi innahu samīʿun ʿalīm
versets.trad: Whenever an evil prompting from Satan stirs you, seek refuge in God. Truly, He hears all, knows all.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: “seek refuge in God” (istiʿādha) — against the subtle assault of Satan there is no weapon of one's own, only recourse to God. To acknowledge one's powerlessness and take refuge in Him is already the victory; for the enemy flees wherever the divine Name is invoked.

mots.racine: س م ع / ع ل م

versets.ar: إِنَّ الَّذِينَ اتَّقَوْا إِذَا مَسَّهُمْ طَائِفٌ مِّنَ الشَّيْطَانِ تَذَكَّرُوا فَإِذَا هُم مُّبْصِرُونَ
versets.tr: inna lladhīna ttaqaw idhā massahum ṭāʾifun mina sh-shayṭāni tadhakkarū fa-idhā hum mubṣirūn
versets.trad: Those who fear God, when a prompting from Satan touches them, remember [the Reminder] and at once they see clearly.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: the mark of the God-fearing: scarcely brushed by temptation, they remember and “at once they see clearly” (mubṣirūn). The dhikr is the lightning-flash that scatters the darkness of the prompting; the watchful heart turns the assault into an occasion of awakening. The remembrance of God restores sight.

versets.ar: وَإِخْوَانُهُمْ يَمُدُّونَهُمْ فِي الْغَيِّ ثُمَّ لَا يُقْصِرُونَ
versets.tr: wa-ikhwānuhum yamuddūnahum fī l-ghayyi thumma lā yuqṣirūn
versets.trad: But their brethren are drawn ever deeper into error, and they never cease.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: while the God-fearing recovers himself, the “brethren” of the devils are dragged ever further into error, without respite (lā yuqṣirūn). Two opposite destinies before the same temptation: the one awakens and sees, the other sinks and is blinded. The difference lies in the remembrance.

versets.ar: وَإِذَا لَمْ تَأْتِهِم بِآيَةٍ قَالُوا لَوْلَا اجْتَبَيْتَهَا ۚ قُلْ إِنَّمَا أَتَّبِعُ مَا يُوحَىٰ إِلَيَّ مِن رَّبِّي ۚ هَٰذَا بَصَائِرُ مِن رَّبِّكُمْ وَهُدًى وَرَحْمَةٌ لِّقَوْمٍ يُؤْمِنُونَ
versets.tr: wa-idhā lam taʾtihim bi-āyatin qālū lawlā jtabaytahā qul innamā attabiʿu mā yūḥā ilayya min rabbī hādhā baṣāʾiru min rabbikum wa-hudan wa-raḥmatun li-qawmin yuʾminūn
versets.trad: When you do not bring them a sign, they say: “Why have you not produced one?” Say: “I only follow what is revealed to me by my Lord.” This is a body of insights from your Lord, a guidance and a mercy for a people who believe.
themes: the Qurʾān, light of the heart
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: the Prophet does not fashion the signs, he follows what is revealed to him; the servant does not dispose, he receives. And the Qurʾān itself is called “insights” (baṣāʾir): it is a light that opens the inner eye. For the believer it is guidance and mercy; the very Book that blinds the denier illumines the open heart.

mots.sens: have you not produced, chosen it

mots.racine: ه د ي / ر ح م

versets.ar: وَإِذَا قُرِئَ الْقُرْآنُ فَاسْتَمِعُوا لَهُ وَأَنصِتُوا لَعَلَّكُمْ تُرْحَمُونَ
versets.tr: wa-idhā quriʾa l-qurʾānu fa-stamiʿū lahu wa-anṣitū laʿallakum turḥamūn
versets.trad: When the Qurʾān is recited, listen to it attentively and be silent, that perhaps mercy may be shown to you.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: “listen and be silent” — true listening (istimāʿ) demands a twofold silence: that of the tongue and that of the heart, so that the Word may descend unhindered. Silence is not emptiness but reception; and mercy pours down upon the one who has fallen still to let Him speak.

versets.ar: وَاذْكُر رَّبَّكَ فِي نَفْسِكَ تَضَرُّعًا وَخِيفَةً وَدُونَ الْجَهْرِ مِنَ الْقَوْلِ بِالْغُدُوِّ وَالْآصَالِ وَلَا تَكُن مِّنَ الْغَافِلِينَ
versets.tr: wa-dhkur rabbaka fī nafsika taḍarruʿan wa-khīfatan wa-dūna l-jahri mina l-qawli bi-l-ghuduwwi wa-l-āṣāli wa-lā takun mina l-ghāfilīn
versets.trad: Remember your Lord within yourself, in humility and awe, without raising your voice, at morning and at evening. And do not be among the heedless.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: “remember your Lord within yourself” — the highest dhikr is that of the heart, silent, “without raising the voice”; it needs no witness, for its sole witness is God. Morning and evening, the soul abides in this secret remembrance, between humility (taḍarruʿ) and awe (khīfa). Not to be among the heedless is never to let the heart absent itself from His presence.

mots.racine: غ د و / أ ص ل

versets.ar: إِنَّ الَّذِينَ عِندَ رَبِّكَ لَا يَسْتَكْبِرُونَ عَنْ عِبَادَتِهِ وَيُسَبِّحُونَهُ وَلَهُ يَسْجُدُونَ ۩
versets.tr: inna lladhīna ʿinda rabbika lā yastakbirūna ʿan ʿibādatihi wa-yusabbiḥūnahu wa-lahu yasjudūn
versets.trad: Those who are with your Lord do not, out of pride, disdain to worship Him. They glorify Him, and before Him they prostrate.
themes: the prostration of those drawn near
versets.commentaire: Verse of prostration that seals the sura. Qushayrī: “those who are with your Lord” are the angels and the chosen hearts admitted to His nearness; and the sign of true nearness is not pride but humility: the closer one is, the lower one bows. Thus the sura that opened upon the proud refusal of Iblīs ends upon the unwearying prostration of those drawn near — from the revolt of the veiled to the worship of the unveiled, the whole journey of the heart is traced.

mots.sens: they do not disdain through pride
